items.contract: Honcho-compatible tool schemas expose GONCHO scope and source allowlist controls without renaming public tools
items.degraded_mode: Memory status and tool schema evidence show when user-scope or source-filtered recall is unavailable.
ready_when: internal/goncho SearchParams and ContextParams already accept scope and sources fields.
not_ready_when: The slice renames public honcho_* tools, changes internal goncho storage, or bundles deny-path/operator evidence work.
acceptance: honcho_search and honcho_context JSON Schemas include optional scope and sources fields.
scope and sources are not required and omitted calls preserve same-chat default behavior.
The internal implementation package and tables remain named goncho while external tool names remain honcho_*.
items.note: TDD landed: `honcho_search` and `honcho_context` keep their public Honcho-compatible names while their JSON Schemas now advertise optional `scope` and `sources` controls backed by the existing internal GONCHO params. Schema tests assert both fields are discoverable and not required, and executor round-trips without either field preserve same-chat default behavior. Deny-path fixtures and operator evidence remain separate 3.E.7 slices.
done_signal: Honcho-compatible tool schema tests prove scope and sources are optional, discoverable, and routed through existing GONCHO params.

items.name: Honcho host integration compatibility fixtures
items.contract: GONCHO preserves host-facing Honcho session, peer, and tool semantics needed by current OpenCode and SillyTavern integrations
items.degraded_mode: Doctor and memory status explain which Honcho-compatible host mappings are unsupported instead of silently accepting incompatible config.
ready_when: Public honcho_* schemas expose scope/source controls and interrupted turns cannot persist partial memory observations.
not_ready_when: The slice adds a cloud Honcho client or changes internal package names instead of fixture-locking host-facing compatibility.
acceptance: Fixtures cover per-directory, per-repo, per-session, chat-instance, and global session strategies as mapping inputs.
Host-scoped config keys do not mutate another host's settings.
GONCHO keeps internal names while generated tool schemas and docs preserve honcho_* external compatibility.
items.note: TDD landed: internal/goncho host integration fixtures now map Hermes/OpenCode/SillyTavern-style workspace, peer, session strategy, recall mode, and Honcho-compatible external tool naming semantics without adding a cloud Honcho client or renaming the internal Goncho service. Fixtures cover per-directory, per-repo, per-session, chat-instance, and global session keys, context/tools/hybrid recall behavior including context-only and tool-only aliases, host-scoped config patch isolation, and fail-closed diagnostics for unsupported host mappings.
done_signal: Host integration fixtures prove GONCHO maps current Honcho host/session concepts without renaming external honcho_* tools.

items.name: SillyTavern persona and group-chat mapping fixtures
items.contract: Goncho maps Honcho SillyTavern peer modes, session naming, enrichment modes, and group-chat participants without widening recall or leaking the internal goncho name
items.degraded_mode: Host mapping evidence reports unsupported SillyTavern panel knobs or missing peer/session identifiers instead of silently merging personas or group characters.
ready_when: Honcho host integration compatibility fixtures already map baseline SillyTavern chat-instance sessions, recall modes, host-scoped config, and honcho_* external tool names.
not_ready_when: The slice adds a SillyTavern extension, imports Node/browser code, calls hosted Honcho, or changes Goncho persistence instead of fixture-locking host mapping decisions.
acceptance: Peer mode fixtures distinguish one shared user peer from separate persona-scoped peers without changing the default Goncho workspace.
Session naming fixtures cover auto per-chat, per-character, and custom session names, including reset/orphan evidence for new chats.
Group-chat fixtures register one peer per character and lazy-add mid-chat characters instead of collapsing to one group peer.
Context-only, reasoning, and tool-call enrichment modes map to prompt context, honcho_chat-style reasoning, and tool exposure with explicit degraded evidence for unsupported modes.
items.note: TDD landed: `internal/goncho/sillytavern_mapping_test.go` fixture-locks Honcho SillyTavern peer modes, auto/per-character/custom session naming with reset/orphan evidence, group-chat one-peer-per-character mapping with lazy mid-chat additions, context/reasoning/tool-call enrichment mapping, and degraded evidence for unsupported panel knobs or missing peer/session identifiers. The mapper stays pure Goncho host evidence: no SillyTavern extension, no Node/browser imports, no hosted Honcho calls, no persistence changes, and external tool evidence preserves honcho_* names including honcho_chat.
done_signal: SillyTavern mapping fixtures prove persona/session/group-chat decisions and enrichment-mode degradation while external tools keep honcho_* names.

provenance.note: Honcho defines host-facing SillyTavern behavior; Gormes maps it into internal Goncho fixtures and operator evidence.

items.name: Cross-chat deny-path fixtures
items.contract: Same-chat default recall with explicit user-scope widening
items.degraded_mode: Memory status and operator evidence report unresolved, conflicting, or denied cross-chat identity bindings.
ready_when: Honcho-compatible scope/source tool schema is complete and exposes source allowlist semantics.
not_ready_when: Deny-path fixtures are mixed with operator evidence rendering in the same slice.
acceptance: Unknown user bindings stay same-chat.
Conflicting user bindings deny user-scope widening.
Allowed user-scope searches include source allowlist evidence.
items.note: TDD landed: recall and session-search fixtures now deny user-scope widening when the current chat binding is unknown, unresolved, or conflicting; denied paths fall back to same-chat/session behavior, and allowed user-scope GONCHO hits preserve origin_source evidence for source allowlists.
done_signal: Cross-chat recall fixtures prove unknown and conflicting identities cannot widen search scope.

items.name: Cross-chat operator evidence
items.contract: Memory and Goncho status expose operator-readable evidence for allowed, denied, and degraded cross-chat recall decisions
items.degraded_mode: Status and dry-run output report unresolved user bindings, conflicting chat bindings, source allowlists, and host mapping gaps before recall widens.
ready_when: Cross-chat deny-path fixtures are complete, Honcho-compatible scope/source tool schemas are visible, and SillyTavern host mapping drift is either fixture-locked or explicitly degraded.
not_ready_when: The slice changes recall ranking, creates new memory storage tables, or ports dialectic reasoning instead of only rendering existing allow/deny evidence.
blocked_by: SillyTavern persona and group-chat mapping fixtures
acceptance: Allowed user-scope recall reports resolved user_id, source allowlist, session/chat source, and number of widened sessions considered.
Denied recall reports unknown, unresolved, or conflicting identity reason and names the same-chat fallback scope.
Goncho context/search dry-run output preserves origin_source evidence for returned hits.
`gormes memory status` or an equivalent diagnostics path renders the evidence without exposing raw SQL rows.
items.note: TDD landed: internal/memory/cross_chat_operator_evidence_test.go pins allowed and denied cross-chat recall evidence with resolved user_id, source allowlists, session/chat source, current binding details, widened-session counts, and same-chat fallback reasons for unresolved, unknown, or conflicting identity. Goncho search/context results now carry scope_evidence and preserve origin_source on returned hits, while gormes goncho doctor --scope user --sources renders the evidence without exposing raw SQL rows. The SillyTavern persona/group-chat mapping gate was fixture-locked before this closeout, so host mapping gaps remain explicit degraded evidence instead of being hidden by generic cross-chat status.
done_signal: Cross-chat evidence fixtures prove allowed and denied recall paths produce operator-readable user/source/session diagnostics.

provenance.note: Gormes owns the operator evidence surface; upstream Honcho/Hermes compatibility defines the host and tool semantics that evidence must not obscure.

drift_state.origin_decision: Honcho e659b6b added SillyTavern integration guidance, and Gormes already has validated host-integration, SillyTavern persona/group mapping, scope/source schema, deny-path, and operator-evidence fixtures. Internal implementation remains Goncho; external compatibility keeps honcho_* naming.

items.name: parent_session_id lineage for compression splits
items.contract: Session metadata records compression/fork lineage and can resolve the live descendant without rewriting ancestor history
items.degraded_mode: Session mirrors and status show missing, orphaned, or looped lineage instead of silently resuming stale roots.
ready_when: internal/session.Metadata already persists source, chat_id, user_id, and updated_at on bbolt-backed sessions.
not_ready_when: The slice implements compression itself or rewrites existing session IDs instead of adding append-only lineage metadata.
acceptance: session.Metadata carries parent_session_id and lineage_kind while root sessions remain parentless by default.
Self-parent and trivial parent loops are rejected in BoltMap and MemMap tests.
SessionIndexMirror or an equivalent read model exposes parent/child/orphan lineage for operator audit.
items.note: Complete: internal/session.Metadata now persists parent_session_id plus lineage_kind, roots default to primary without a parent, BoltMap and MemMap reject self-parent and parent-chain loops, ResolveLineageTip walks append-only compression descendants without rewriting ancestor metadata, and SessionIndexMirror renders child/orphan lineage status for operator audit. Compression execution, gateway resume routing, and lineage-aware search remain separate dependent rows.
done_signal: Session lineage fixtures prove append-only parent metadata, loop rejection, and operator-visible orphan status without implementing compression.

items.name: Gateway resume follows compression continuation
items.contract: Gateway and CLI resume resolve a titled or root session to the newest live compression descendant before loading transcript history
items.degraded_mode: Resume status reports unresolved continuation chains and falls back visibly instead of loading an ended compression root as live history.
ready_when: Session lineage metadata can resolve root -> child chains and distinguish ended compression roots from live descendants.
not_ready_when: The slice changes context compression behavior or loads transcripts from a separate store instead of reusing the native session read model.
acceptance: Resume by title or root ID follows a compression child chain to the newest live descendant.
Already-current session checks compare against the resolved descendant, not the stale root.
Transcript loading and session switching use the resolved descendant while operator output still names the requested session.
items.note: Complete: TDD added internal/gateway/resume_continuation_test.go proving stored resume roots follow compression lineage to the newest live descendant before gateway submit, fork children are ignored, unresolved continuation chains fall back to the requested root with visible resume status, and the session context still names the requested stale root while using the resolved descendant as the live SessionID.
done_signal: Gateway resume fixtures prove compressed roots reopen live descendants and stale roots are reported visibly.

items.name: Source-filtered session/message search core
items.note: TDD landed: internal/memory.SearchMessages and SearchSessions search canonical user_id-bound sessions with optional source allowlists, deterministic latest-turn ordering, and split source/chat key normalization.

items.name: GONCHO user-scope search/context parameters
items.note: TDD landed: internal/goncho SearchParams and ContextParams accept scope=user plus sources[] and pass them into the session catalog; the Honcho-compatible tool schemas still need to advertise those fields.

items.name: Lineage-aware source-filtered search hits
items.contract: Session and message search can surface parent/child lineage context for matched sessions without widening the same-chat default recall fence
items.degraded_mode: Search evidence reports lineage unavailable or orphaned instead of implying a complete compression chain.
ready_when: Session lineage metadata is persisted and can be queried from the session read model.
not_ready_when: The slice changes user-scope access rules or bundles operator evidence rendering in the same implementation.
acceptance: SearchMessages and SearchSessions include lineage metadata for matched sessions when parent/child data exists.
Same-chat default recall remains unchanged unless scope=user or source filters explicitly widen it.
Orphaned or incomplete chains produce explicit evidence fields instead of dropped results.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed internal/memory/session_lineage_search_test.go. SearchMessages and SearchSessions now attach SearchLineage evidence with parent_session_id, lineage_kind, child_session_ids, and explicit ok/orphan/unavailable status without changing the same-chat default recall fence.
done_signal: Lineage-aware search fixtures prove matched sessions carry parent/child context while default same-chat recall stays fenced.

items.name: Operator-auditable search evidence
items.contract: Goncho/Honcho-compatible search surfaces operator evidence for user scope, source filters, and session lineage on every widened cross-source search hit
items.degraded_mode: Search output reports missing session-directory evidence, same-chat fallback, unavailable lineage, orphaned lineage, and source allowlists instead of implying cross-source recall was safe by default.
ready_when: Lineage-aware source-filtered search hits are validated on main.
GONCHO user-scope search/context parameters and Honcho-compatible scope/source tool schemas are validated on main.
The slice can be tested from seeded SQLite/Bolt fixtures without provider calls or external Honcho services.
not_ready_when: The slice changes search ranking, widens default same-chat recall, implements Honcho v3 HTTP/SDK parity, or adds new filters beyond reporting existing scope/source/lineage evidence.
acceptance: `gormes goncho doctor --peer <user> --session <source:chat> --scope user --sources <source>` text output includes scope decision, source allowlist, sessions considered, widened session count, search hit source/origin_source/session_key, and lineage status for each hit.
JSON output includes the same search evidence fields so autoloop and docs tests can assert them without parsing text.
Search evidence reports orphan/unavailable lineage explicitly and does not widen recall when scope=user is denied or session directory evidence is missing.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed cmd/gormes/goncho_search_evidence_test.go with seeded current, child, orphan, and chat-only sessions. Goncho search results now thread memory.SearchLineage through goncho.SearchHit into doctor text/JSON evidence, including source allowlists, user-scope decisions, sessions considered, widened session counts, orphan/unavailable lineage statuses, and same-chat fallback evidence when the session directory is missing, without changing search ranking or default recall fences.
done_signal: Goncho doctor fixtures prove cross-source search evidence names source filters, user-scope decisions, and lineage status for widened hits without changing default recall fences.

provenance.note: Honcho v3 defines search/filter concepts; Gormes owns the operator-evidence shape that makes Goncho cross-source search auditable inside one local binary.

items.name: Goncho context representation options
items.contract: honcho_context exposes the Honcho v3 session.context representation controls while preserving current same-chat defaults
items.degraded_mode: Unsupported representation options return structured unavailable evidence instead of being silently ignored.
ready_when: honcho_context already accepts peer, query, max_tokens, session_key, scope, and sources through the Goncho service.
not_ready_when: The slice attempts to implement summaries, observations, or dialectic reasoning in the same change.
unblocks: Directional peer cards and representation scopes
acceptance: ContextParams and the honcho_context JSON Schema expose optional peer_target, peer_perspective, limit_to_session, search_top_k, search_max_distance, include_most_frequent, and max_conclusions fields.
Omitted fields preserve current same-chat context behavior.
limit_to_session=true cannot widen recall through scope=user.
Fields that need the future observation table report explicit unsupported evidence.
items.note: TDD landed: ContextParams and the honcho_context schema expose optional peer_target, peer_perspective, limit_to_session, search_top_k, search_max_distance, include_most_frequent, and max_conclusions. Omitted fields preserve same-chat context defaults; limit_to_session=true fails closed without session_key and cannot widen through scope=user; unsupported directional and semantic representation options return structured unavailable evidence. Summaries, observations, and dialectic-backed retrieval remain separate slices.
done_signal: honcho_context schema and service fixtures prove Honcho v3 context options are discoverable, optional, and visibly degraded when not implemented.

items.name: Goncho search filter grammar
items.contract: Goncho search accepts a typed subset of Honcho v3 filters and rejects unsupported filter operators visibly
items.degraded_mode: Unknown filters, unsupported operators, or metadata paths return a structured unsupported-filter error instead of widening search.
ready_when: Same-chat and user-scope deny paths are fixture-locked so filter failures cannot accidentally widen recall.
not_ready_when: The slice adds an HTTP surface or full SDK compatibility before the internal filter AST is tested.
acceptance: Filter AST fixtures cover AND, OR, NOT, gt, gte, lt, lte, ne, in, contains, icontains, metadata, and wildcard parsing.
The first executable implementation supports a documented subset and returns unsupported-filter evidence for the rest.
Limit defaults to 10 and clamps to the Honcho-documented maximum of 100.
items.note: TDD landed: Goncho SearchParams now accept Honcho-shaped filters and limit, parse an internal AST for AND, OR, NOT, gt, gte, lt, lte, ne, in, contains, icontains, metadata paths, and wildcard values, then compile only the current enforceable subset. session_id, source, and peer_id equality/in/wildcard filters narrow search; metadata, content, created_at, OR, NOT, unknown fields, and unknown operators return structured unsupported-filter errors instead of being ignored. Same-chat source/session deny paths and user-scope session/source filters are fixture-locked, and search limit defaults to 10 with a maximum of 100.
done_signal: Filter grammar tests prove unsupported Honcho filters fail closed and supported filters keep workspace/user/session boundaries intact.

items.name: Directional peer cards and representation scopes
items.contract: Peer cards and stored representations are keyed by workspace, observer, and observed peer instead of a flat workspace/peer pair
items.degraded_mode: When directional representation is unavailable, the service reports that only the default gormes observer view was used.
ready_when: Context options expose observer/target fields and current peer-card replacement behavior is fixture-locked.
not_ready_when: The slice tries to port observe_others scheduling before the storage key and card semantics are stable.
acceptance: Peer cards enforce Honcho's max-40-facts cap.
Manual set_card behavior replaces the full card instead of merging.
Directional target cards are stored independently from default self cards.
Late-join non-retroactive reasoning remains documented as unsupported until the deriver queue exists.
items.note: TDD landed: peer cards are stored by workspace, observer, and observed peer with legacy flat cards migrated to observer=gormes. Directional fixtures prove observer/observed isolation, max-40 truncation, and manual set_card replacement semantics; honcho_profile exposes optional target for directional card reads/writes. Context still reports directional representation as unavailable and uses only the default gormes observer view. Late-join non-retroactive reasoning and observe_others scheduling remain unsupported until the deriver queue exists.
done_signal: Directional peer-card fixtures prove observer/observed isolation, max-card cap, and replacement semantics.

items.name: Goncho queue status read model
items.contract: Gormes exposes Honcho-style representation, summary, and dream work-unit queue status as observability, not synchronization
items.degraded_mode: If no Goncho task queue exists yet, memory status reports zero tracked Goncho work units plus the existing extractor queue status.
ready_when: At least one Goncho-owned task type or a zero-state read model is available to report deterministically.
not_ready_when: The slice waits for the queue to drain or treats queue empty as an application synchronization condition.
blocked_by: Directional peer cards and representation scopes
acceptance: Status fields include completed_work_units, in_progress_work_units, pending_work_units, total_work_units, and optional per-session details.
Only representation, summary, and dream task types count toward Honcho-style queue status.
Docs and CLI output state that queue status is for observability and debugging, not waiting for completion.
items.note: TDD landed: Goncho exposes a Honcho-style zero-state queue status read model for representation, summary, and dream work units with completed_work_units, in_progress_work_units, pending_work_units, total_work_units, and optional per-session details. Memory status and Goncho doctor output include extractor queue status alongside Goncho work-unit counts and explicitly frame queue status as observability/debugging evidence, not a synchronization contract or queue-drain wait condition.
done_signal: Queue status fixtures prove Honcho-style counts and document that queue empty is not a synchronization contract.

items.name: Goncho summary context budget
items.contract: Session summaries use Honcho's short/long cadence and 40/60 context budget without double-billing last-N-turn recall
items.degraded_mode: When summaries are unavailable or too large for the token budget, context returns recent messages plus explicit summary_absent evidence.
ready_when: Context options are schema-visible and the memory store can add a session_summaries table via migration.
not_ready_when: The slice rewrites RecallProvider.GetContext or merges summaries into the existing memory-context fence instead of adding a separate Goncho context component.
acceptance: Schema stores one short and one long summary slot per session with last-covered message and token count.
Short summaries trigger every 20 messages and long summaries every 60 messages by default.
Service.Context allocates 40 percent of the token budget to summary and 60 percent to recent messages when summary=true.
summary=false fills the budget with recent messages only.
items.note: TDD landed: Goncho now stores one short and one long session summary slot in goncho_session_summaries, refreshes deterministic short/long slots at the Honcho default 20/60 message cadence, exposes summary/tokens/search_query context options, allocates summary=true context with a 40 percent summary budget and 60 percent recent-message budget without reusing messages covered by the selected summary, lets summary=false spend the token budget on recent messages only, and reports summary_absent evidence when no requested summary can fit.
done_signal: Summary context fixtures prove short/long cadence, token-budget allocation, summary=false behavior, and visible degradation when no summary can fit.

items.name: Goncho dialectic chat contract
items.contract: Goncho exposes a Honcho peer.chat-compatible request and response contract while keeping query-specific reasoning separate from prompt-time context assembly
items.degraded_mode: Until the real dialectic tool loop and streaming transport land, honcho_chat returns deterministic content plus explicit unsupported evidence for stream=true and target-specific reasoning gaps.
ready_when: Context options are schema-visible and manual conclusions can be queried through the existing Goncho service.
not_ready_when: The slice replaces honcho_context or removes honcho_reasoning instead of adding the host-compatible honcho_chat contract.
acceptance: Chat params accept query, session_id, target, reasoning_level, and stream.
The default reasoning level is low and invalid reasoning levels are rejected.
The non-streaming response shape is content-only and matches Honcho's OpenAPI response contract.
stream=true and target-specific reasoning gaps return explicit degraded evidence until implemented.
honcho_reasoning remains available for backward compatibility.
items.note: Honcho docs expose peer.chat as the slow query-specific reasoning path; newer host integrations call this honcho_chat or chat. Gormes should add the alias and contract before porting the full dialectic tool loop.
done_signal: Chat contract tests prove request validation, default reasoning level, response shape, host-compatible tool alias, and explicit streaming degradation.

items.name: Goncho file upload import ingestion
items.contract: Goncho can import text-like memory files as session messages without persisting original file bytes, matching Honcho's file-upload migration model
items.degraded_mode: Until PDF extraction and a Goncho queue exist, unsupported content types fail before writes and imported messages report queue-unavailable evidence.
ready_when: Goncho has a deterministic session-message write path or a documented queue-unavailable degraded path for imported messages.
not_ready_when: The slice stores original uploaded file bytes, silently accepts unsupported content types, or attempts PDF/OCR extraction before text and JSON imports are fixture-locked.
acceptance: Text, Markdown, and JSON imports create normal session messages with required peer_id.
Imported chunks persist file_id, filename, chunk_index, total_chunks, original_file_size, content_type, and chunk_character_range metadata.
Original file bytes are not persisted.
created_at, metadata, and configuration are preserved when provided.
Runtime chunk size follows Honcho source settings.MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE at 25000 characters unless upstream changes that setting.
items.note: TDD landed: internal/goncho/file_import_test.go covers text, Markdown, and JSON imports as ordinary session messages, file metadata in meta_json, required peer_id, unsupported content-type rejection before writes, no raw JSON file-byte persistence, created_at/metadata/configuration preservation, Honcho MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE chunking at 25000 characters, and queue-unavailable evidence. Verified with go test ./internal/goncho ./internal/memory ./cmd/gormes -count=1.
done_signal: File import fixtures prove supported formats become ordinary messages, unsupported formats fail before writes, and original files are not persisted.

items.name: Goncho topology design fixtures
items.contract: Goncho workspace, peer, session, and observation defaults are fixture-locked before more memory behavior is added
items.degraded_mode: Unknown external participant identity falls back to a deterministic source-prefixed peer ID and records the fallback in evidence.
ready_when: The current session directory, Goncho service types, and Honcho tool schemas are readable in the repo.
not_ready_when: The slice changes persistence behavior or reasoning behavior before topology rules are proven.
unblocks: Directional peer cards and representation scopes
acceptance: Default workspace is gormes unless explicit hard isolation is requested.
Workspace-per-user is rejected in fixtures.
Peer ID selection prefers internal/session.Metadata.UserID and falls back to source-prefixed external IDs.
Deterministic assistants, transport bots, and import helpers default to observe_me=false.
Conversation sessions follow real context boundaries such as thread, channel, repo, import batch, or delegated child run.
Cross-peer observation is opt-in and cannot become a default side effect.
items.note: TDD landed: internal/goncho/topology_design_test.go locks the default gormes workspace, rejects workspace-per-user, proves canonical session user IDs before source-prefixed external fallback IDs with evidence, keeps deterministic assistants/transport bots/import helpers unobserved by default, names real session boundaries, and keeps cross-peer observation opt-in.
done_signal: Topology fixtures prove the default workspace, peer ID derivation, observation defaults, and session boundary choices expected by the operator playbook.

items.name: Goncho operator diagnostics contract
items.contract: Gormes exposes a Honcho-inspired Goncho doctor path that checks memory topology, queues, config, and degraded modes without requiring operators to inspect raw tables
items.degraded_mode: Missing optional model/provider features are reported as degraded capability rows, not startup failures, unless a requested command needs them.
ready_when: Topology rules define the expected workspace, peer, session, and observation defaults.
not_ready_when: The slice reaches out to upstream Honcho, external network services, or hosted LLMs by default.
acceptance: A human-readable command reports config, schema, session catalog, tool registration, context dry-run, queue status, conclusion availability, summary availability, provider readiness, and degraded modes.
A JSON output mode is available for autoloop and planner consumption.
Exit code 0 means healthy or explicitly degraded, 1 means client/config issue, 2 means runtime/storage issue, and 3 means auth/provider issue.
Zero-state databases report no data without failing.
The command reuses existing memory status where possible instead of adding a second status model.
items.note: TDD landed: `gormes goncho doctor` reports config paths, schema/table presence, session catalog state, Honcho-compatible tool registration, context dry-run output, extractor and Goncho queue status, conclusion and summary availability, provider readiness, and visible degraded modes without external Honcho, network services, or hosted LLM calls by default. JSON output is available for autoloop/planner consumption; exit-code fixtures cover 0 healthy/degraded, 1 client/config, 2 runtime/storage, and 3 auth/provider. `gormes memory status` now includes the observability-only Goncho representation/summary/dream zero-state alongside the existing extractor queue.
done_signal: Doctor fixtures prove text and JSON output, exit-code mapping, zero-state behavior, and visible degraded capability reporting.

items.name: Goncho streaming chat persistence contract
items.contract: Goncho streaming chat stores the final assistant response once and never turns partial stream chunks into memory
items.degraded_mode: Until streaming transport exists, stream=true returns explicit unsupported evidence while non-streaming chat keeps the Honcho-compatible response contract.
ready_when: honcho_chat or equivalent dialectic chat params and response shape are fixture-locked.
not_ready_when: The slice stores stream chunks as messages, creates synthetic assistant turns before completion, or changes honcho_context behavior.
unblocks: Hugo docs examples for streaming memory behavior
acceptance: Streaming response handlers accumulate chunks into a final response before persistence.
Partial chunks are never written as memory messages.
Interrupted streams record interruption evidence and do not flush incomplete assistant content.
Successful streamed assistant responses are stored exactly once with the same session and assistant peer as non-streaming chat.
Token/counting metadata can be attached after completion without affecting the stored text.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed internal/goncho/streaming_chat_persistence_test.go. The fixture proves stream=true degraded chat persists the final assistant response once, streaming handlers buffer chunks until completion, token metadata attaches after completion without mutating stored text, and interrupted streams return evidence without flushing partial assistant content to memory.
done_signal: Streaming fixtures prove completed responses are persisted once and interrupted or partial chunks cannot pollute memory.

items.name: Goncho configuration namespace
items.contract: Gormes owns a Go-native [goncho] configuration namespace that maps Honcho runtime limits and feature gates into existing config loading
items.degraded_mode: Unset Goncho config uses documented defaults and reports feature-disabled evidence instead of requiring Honcho-style Python service variables.
ready_when: The existing Gormes config loader and doctor output can be extended without changing unrelated agent settings.
not_ready_when: The slice copies Honcho Python environment variables directly or requires provider credentials before Goncho can run in zero-state mode.
acceptance: Config supports enabled, workspace, observer_peer, recent_messages, max_message_size, max_file_size, get_context_max_tokens, reasoning_enabled, peer_card_enabled, summary_enabled, dream_enabled, deriver_workers, representation_batch_max_tokens, and dialectic_default_level.
Environment variables use the GORMES_GONCHO_ prefix.

Defaults mirror Honcho docs where applicable: max_message_size=25000, max_file_size=5242880, get_context_max_tokens=100000, deriver_workers=1, representation_batch_max_tokens=1024, and dream_enabled=false until fixtures exist.
Invalid reasoning levels and negative limits fail config validation.
Doctor output includes the effective Goncho config with secrets redacted.
items.note: TDD landed: internal/config/goncho_config_test.go proves [goncho] defaults, GORMES_GONCHO_* overrides, invalid dialectic levels, negative limit validation, and mapping into goncho.Config; internal/goncho/config_test.go locks the Go-native runtime defaults and dialectic levels; cmd/gormes/goncho_doctor_test.go proves doctor visibility with secrets absent and dream feature-disabled evidence.
done_signal: Config fixtures prove defaults, GORMES_GONCHO_* overrides, validation failures, and doctor visibility for effective Goncho settings.

items.name: Goncho dreaming scheduler contract
items.contract: Goncho models Honcho dream scheduling as local auditable work intent with cooldown, idle, threshold, and dedupe gates before any LLM dream execution
items.degraded_mode: When dreaming is disabled or no scheduler table exists, Goncho context and doctor output report dream_disabled or dream_unavailable evidence rather than implying background reasoning is active.
ready_when: Goncho configuration namespace, queue status read model, and directional representation scope fixtures are validated on main.
The first slice only records dream work intent and degraded evidence; it does not need a model provider, worker daemon, or surprisal tree.
not_ready_when: The slice runs LLM deduction or induction, implements tree-based surprisal, starts background workers, or calls a hosted Honcho service.
acceptance: Scheduler fixtures create at most one pending or in-progress dream per workspace/observer/observed tuple.
A dream is eligible only after at least 50 new conclusions since the last dream, an eight-hour cooldown, and the configured idle timeout.
New activity cancels or marks stale a pending dream for the observed peer without deleting prior dream history.
Manual schedule requests are deduped and report whether they created, reused, or rejected a dream intent.
Doctor/status output exposes dream_disabled, dream_pending, dream_in_progress, and dream_cooldown evidence without waiting for queue emptiness.
items.note: TDD landed: Goncho now records local auditable dream work intent in goncho_dreams, gates automatic scheduling on 50 new conclusions, eight-hour cooldown, and configured idle timeout, dedupes pending/in-progress work per workspace/observer/observed tuple, marks pending dreams stale on new activity, and reports dream_disabled, dream_unavailable, dream_pending, dream_in_progress, and dream_cooldown in context, doctor, and status evidence without LLM execution, surprisal trees, hosted Honcho calls, or background workers.
done_signal: Dream scheduler fixtures prove threshold, cooldown, idle, dedupe, manual scheduling, cancellation, and doctor/status degraded evidence without LLM execution.

provenance.note: Honcho defines dream eligibility semantics; Gormes keeps the first slice as Go-native Goncho scheduler evidence before any hosted or LLM-backed dream execution.

drift_state.origin_decision: Honcho e659b6b remains the latest synchronized memory donor for core memory docs, and all Goncho/Honcho parity rows are validated on main including host integration, SillyTavern mapping, filter grammar, streaming chat persistence, and Go-native dream scheduler intent. Honcho MCP self-hosted HONCHO_API_URL drift is now tracked under Phase 5.G MCP config/env resolution rather than reopening internal Goncho memory. Keep the implementation named Goncho while preserving honcho_* public tool compatibility.

4.name: Phase 4 — The Brain Transplant
4.deliverable: Native Go agent orchestrator + prompt builder
4.dependency_note: Build priority: 2.E0 deterministic subagent runtime -> 2.G static skills + reviewed candidate flow -> runner-enforced delegation policy + wider gateway surface -> native agent loop

items.name: Provider interface + stream fixture harness
items.contract: Provider-neutral request and stream event transcript harness
items.degraded_mode: Provider status reports missing fixture coverage or unavailable adapters before kernel routing can select them.
ready_when: Anthropic transcript fixtures replay request, stream, finish reason, and usage data without live credentials.
not_ready_when: A provider-specific adapter lands before shared transcript fixtures prove the contract.
unblocks: Bedrock Converse payload mapping (no AWS SDK)
acceptance: Provider transcripts replay request, stream, finish reason, and usage data without live credentials.
EOF after partial tool_call surfaces pending calls instead of dropping them.
Duplicate tool-name deltas cannot concatenate into invented names.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed `internal/hermes/provider_transcript_test.go` plus reusable fixtures under `internal/hermes/testdata/provider_transcripts/`. The harness replays request bodies and SSE transcripts without live credentials, covers Anthropic request shaping, reasoning/text stream events, finish_reason mapping, usage, and tool-call accumulation, and locks the upstream #12072/#0f778f77 regressions by flushing pending OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic tool calls on EOF while proving repeated tool-name deltas do not concatenate into invented names.
done_signal: Provider transcript fixtures replay request, stream, finish reason, and usage data without live credentials.

items.name: Tool-call normalization + continuation contract
items.contract: Cross-provider tool-call continuation contract
items.degraded_mode: Provider status reports transcript or continuation fixture gaps before adapters can be selected for tool-capable turns.
ready_when: Provider interface + stream fixture harness is available for cross-provider tool continuation fixtures.
not_ready_when: Continuation mapping is implemented inside one provider adapter instead of the shared event model.
unblocks: DeepSeek/Kimi reasoning_content echo for tool-call replay
acceptance: Assistant tool-call messages and tool replies round-trip through the shared event model.
Continuation payloads preserve provider-safe finish reasons.
Cross-provider fixtures cover mid-stream stall recovery and duplicate-name accumulator safety.
items.note: Complete: TDD added provider transcript coverage for OpenAI-compatible continuation requests so assistant `ToolCalls` and linked `tool` replies serialize from the shared `hermes.Message` model instead of being dropped to role/content only. The shared harness now proves Anthropic continuation mapping, OpenAI-compatible continuation mapping, provider-safe `tool_calls`/`stop` finish reasons, EOF recovery for partial streamed tool calls, and duplicate-name accumulator safety before Bedrock/Gemini/OpenRouter/Codex reuse the seam.
done_signal: Cross-provider fixtures prove tool-call accumulation and continuation payloads preserve provider-safe semantics.

items.name: DeepSeek/Kimi reasoning_content echo for tool-call replay
items.contract: Thinking-mode providers that require reasoning_content on assistant tool-call turns receive an echoed value during persistence and API replay
items.degraded_mode: Provider status explains when a thinking-mode provider requires reasoning echo padding and when a stored transcript was repaired for replay.
ready_when: Shared provider continuation fixtures can serialize assistant tool-call messages and replay them without live provider credentials.
not_ready_when: The slice stores hidden reasoning text in ordinary assistant content or changes non-thinking providers' replay payloads.
unblocks: Codex stream repair + tool-call leak sanitizer
acceptance: DeepSeek is detected by provider name, model substring, or api.deepseek.com host and gets reasoning_content="" on assistant tool-call replay when no reasoning exists.
Kimi/Moonshot detection keeps the existing reasoning_content padding behavior.
Explicit reasoning_content or reasoning fields are preserved, while non-tool assistant turns and non-thinking providers are left untouched.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed `internal/hermes/reasoning_content_echo_test.go` with provider-boundary fixtures proving DeepSeek and Kimi/Moonshot OpenAI-compatible replays inject `reasoning_content=""` on assistant tool-call messages when no reasoning exists. Detection covers DeepSeek provider name, model substring, and `api.deepseek.com` host plus Kimi/Moonshot provider/host signals. Explicit `reasoning_content` and normalized `reasoning` echoes are preserved on thinking-provider tool-call turns, non-tool assistant turns and generic providers are left unpadded, ordinary assistant content is unchanged, and provider status exposes the reasoning_content padding/repair degraded mode.
done_signal: Reasoning echo fixtures prove DeepSeek and Kimi tool-call replays include provider-required reasoning_content without mutating ordinary assistant content.

items.name: DeepSeek/Kimi cross-provider reasoning isolation
items.contract: DeepSeek and Kimi replay of assistant tool-call turns injects an empty reasoning_content placeholder before promoting generic stored reasoning from another provider
items.degraded_mode: Provider status reports cross-provider reasoning isolation as unavailable until replay fixtures prove prior-provider reasoning is not forwarded to DeepSeek/Kimi tool-call continuations.
ready_when: DeepSeek/Kimi reasoning_content echo for tool-call replay is validated, so provider detection and basic empty-placeholder behavior already exist.
The slice can use the existing captureRequestHTTPClient fixture; no live provider credentials or provider adapter rewrites are required.
not_ready_when: The slice changes non-thinking provider payloads, strips explicit same-provider reasoning_content, or stores hidden reasoning text in ordinary assistant content.
The slice attempts OpenRouter, Codex Responses, Bedrock, or generic reasoning-tag sanitization behavior beyond the DeepSeek/Kimi replay ordering bug.
unblocks: Codex stream repair + tool-call leak sanitizer
acceptance: A DeepSeek replay fixture with an assistant tool-call message containing generic Reasoning but no explicit ReasoningContent sends reasoning_content="" instead of the generic reasoning text.
The same isolation fixture passes for Kimi/Moonshot provider detection.
An explicit ReasoningContent string on the assistant tool-call message is still preserved verbatim.
Non-tool assistant turns and non-thinking providers still omit reasoning_content and the storage-only reasoning field from outgoing API payloads.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed DeepSeek/Kimi cross-provider reasoning isolation in `internal/hermes/reasoning_content_echo_test.go` and `internal/hermes/http_client.go`. Replay fixtures now prove provider-native `ReasoningContent` is preserved, but generic storage-only `Message.Reasoning` from a prior provider is not forwarded to DeepSeek/Kimi/Moonshot assistant tool-call continuations; those turns receive `reasoning_content=""` instead. Non-tool assistant turns and non-thinking provider payloads still omit `reasoning_content` and never serialize the storage-only `reasoning` field.
done_signal: Reasoning echo fixtures prove DeepSeek/Kimi replay sends empty reasoning_content for prior-provider generic reasoning, preserves explicit reasoning_content, and leaves non-thinking payloads untouched.

provenance.note: Hermes changed provider replay ordering to avoid cross-provider reasoning leakage; Gormes should port the behavior as a focused provider-boundary fixture.

items.name: DeepSeek/Kimi all-assistant reasoning_content replay
items.contract: DeepSeek, Kimi, and Moonshot OpenAI-compatible replays include provider-required reasoning_content on every assistant message while preserving Gormes' cross-provider reasoning isolation
items.degraded_mode: Provider status reports all-assistant reasoning_content padding as unavailable until fixtures prove plain assistant turns and tool-call turns both satisfy thinking-provider replay requirements.
ready_when: DeepSeek/Kimi cross-provider reasoning isolation is validated, so provider detection and storage-only reasoning isolation already exist.
The existing captureRequestHTTPClient fixture can inspect serialized OpenAI-compatible request messages without live provider credentials.
The prior worker branch reached provider implementation and only needed the known asynchronous memory test wait helper stabilization during pre-promotion repair.
not_ready_when: The slice forwards storage-only Message.Reasoning from a prior provider to DeepSeek/Kimi/Moonshot instead of using provider-native ReasoningContent or an empty placeholder.
The slice changes non-thinking provider payloads, provider routing, or Codex/Bedrock/Gemini request builders.

Full-suite instability requires memory behavior changes beyond stabilizing internal/memory/interrupted_sync_test.go asynchronous waits; split a separate memory-stability row instead of widening this provider row.
unblocks: Codex stream repair + tool-call leak sanitizer
acceptance: A DeepSeek/Moonshot replay fixture with a plain assistant text turn and no ReasoningContent serializes reasoning_content="".
Assistant tool-call turns still serialize reasoning_content="" when no provider-native ReasoningContent exists.
Explicit provider-native ReasoningContent is preserved on plain and tool-call assistant turns.
Storage-only Message.Reasoning is not serialized to thinking providers, and non-thinking providers remain unchanged.
items.note: Complete: TDD widened the OpenAI-compatible reasoning_content replay fixture and serializer so DeepSeek/Kimi/Moonshot assistant messages always carry provider-required `reasoning_content`. Plain assistant turns and assistant tool-call turns without provider-native `ReasoningContent` serialize `reasoning_content=""`; explicit provider-native `ReasoningContent` is preserved on both shapes; storage-only `Message.Reasoning` remains isolated from thinking-provider payloads; and non-thinking provider payloads remain unchanged.
done_signal: Reasoning echo fixtures prove plain assistant and tool-call assistant messages satisfy DeepSeek/Kimi/Moonshot reasoning_content replay without leaking storage-only reasoning.

items.name: Azure Foundry probe — path sniffing
items.contract: TDD packet for a missing pure helper; create exactly two files and do not edit the already validated /models probe. First add internal/hermes/azure_foundry_path_sniff_test.go in package hermes with TestClassifyAzurePath table subtests named exact_anthropic, trailing_anthropic, nested_anthropic, mixed_case, bare_host_unknown, openai_path_unknown, substring_false_positives, and empty_and_malformed_unknown. Then add internal/hermes/azure_foundry_path_sniff.go exposing ClassifyAzurePath(rawURL string) AzureTransport that reuses the AzureTransport type/constants from internal/hermes/azure_foundry_models_probe.go. Algorithm: url.Parse; empty path or parse error => AzureTransportUnknown; lowercase and strings.TrimRight(parsed.Path, "/"); return AzureTransportAnthropic only when the path equals "/anthropic", ends with "/anthropic", or contains "/anthropic/". This slice never returns AzureTransportOpenAI and never opens HTTP, reads env/config, writes files, starts goroutines, or changes ProbeAzureFoundry.
items.degraded_mode: Probe status reports azure_path_sniff_unknown when no path heuristic matches, and azure_path_sniff_evidence with detected scheme/host/path otherwise.
ready_when: internal/hermes/azure_foundry_models_probe.go is complete and owns the AzureTransport type/constant values; this row only adds a path-sniff helper file plus a sibling _test.go.
No upstream gating: this is a pure URL inspector with synthetic input.

If ClassifyAzurePath already exists in the worker checkout, the worker should run the focused test and update this progress row instead of adding a second detector.
not_ready_when: The slice opens HTTP connections, performs a /models probe, reads AZURE_FOUNDRY_BASE_URL or AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY, or mutates config.
The slice introduces detection of any third transport family (Bedrock, Vertex, etc.).

TestClassifyAzurePath_CaseInsensitive: /AnthrOPic and /ANTHROPIC both return AzureTransportAnthropic.
TestClassifyAzurePath_OpenAIDefault: https://x.openai.azure.com/openai/v1/chat/completions returns AzureTransportUnknown (never AzureTransportOpenAI in this slice).
TestClassifyAzurePath_MalformedReturnsUnknown: empty string, "::garbage::", and rawURL="http://%zz" return AzureTransportUnknown.

items.name: Azure Foundry probe — /models classification + Anthropic fallback
items.contract: Pure helper internal/hermes/azure_foundry_models_probe.go exposes type AzureProbeResult struct{Transport AzureTransport; Models []string; Reason string; Evidence []string} and ProbeAzureFoundry(ctx, client *http.Client, base, apiKey string) (AzureProbeResult, error). Probes <base>/models first; on 200 + OpenAI-shaped JSON returns openai_chat_completions with the model IDs. On non-200 or shape mismatch, probes <base>/v1/messages with a zero-token Anthropic Messages payload and classifies anthropic_messages on any 4xx that mentions 'messages' or 'model'. Otherwise returns Transport=unknown and Reason=manual_required. Tests use httptest.Server fakes only — no live Azure call, no credential storage, no config writes.

items.name: Bedrock
items.note: Umbrella only: not landed on main. Five codexu/* worker attempts on 2026-04-23 produced candidate Bedrock adapters (`feat(phase4): add bedrock provider adapter` x2, `feat(provider): add bedrock client`, `feat(provider): add native Bedrock adapter`, `feat(gormes): add native bedrock provider adapter`) but none merged — no `bedrock_*.go` file is tracked in `internal/hermes/` and `grep -i bedrock|converse[sS]tream` over that package returns zero matches. Execute via the three dependency-ordered TDD slices below (payload mapping -> stream decoding -> SigV4/credential seam) before another full-rewrite attempt.

The mapper converts shared system/user/assistant/tool-result messages into Converse roles and content blocks, preserves assistant reasoning blocks and Bedrock cachePoint hints, maps tool definitions to `toolSpec.inputSchema.json` without dropping required fields, and pins `inferenceConfig.maxTokens`/`temperature` plus empty-content placeholders without importing AWS SDK clients or signing live requests. Bedrock remains unavailable until stream decoding and credential wiring land.

Requires the payload-mapping and stream-decoding fixtures to be green first; unit tests must cover signing-determinism, missing-credential failure, and region-override selection without network calls.

items.name: OpenRouter
items.note: Not landed on main. Five codexu/* worker attempts on 2026-04-23 (`343ec085` / `c009045d` / `e7b271a7` / `f00010b1` / `b3b0c366`) produced candidate OpenRouter adapters but none merged — no `openrouter_*.go` file is tracked in `internal/hermes/` and `grep -i openrouter` over that package returns zero matches. Prior planner note incorrectly cited `agent/openrouter_client.py`, which does not exist upstream: `tools/openrouter_client.py` is only a 33-line async-client getter that delegates to `agent/auxiliary_client.py`. TDD scope: OpenRouter is not a standalone adapter upstream — it is one of several OpenAI-compatible providers routed through the shared provider resolver. Pin the behavior by porting against the real upstream surfaces: `agent/auxiliary_client.py:815 _try_openrouter`, `agent/auxiliary_client.py:2069 _is_openrouter_client`, the `X-OpenRouter-Title` / `X-OpenRouter-Categories` / `HTTP-Referer` app-attribution headers around `agent/auxiliary_client.py:170`, `agent/model_metadata.py:214 _is_openrouter_base_url`, `agent/usage_pricing.py:411 _openrouter_pricing_entry`, and `hermes_cli/runtime_provider.py:429 _resolve_openrouter_runtime`. Fixture-only unit tests must prove (a) base URL and model-family selection over the OpenAI-compatible client seam, (b) attribution-header emission, (c) retryable vs fatal error mapping through `internal/hermes/errors.go`, and (d) usage/pricing accounting against captured response fixtures — no live `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in tests. Land after `Provider interface + stream fixture harness` and `Tool-call normalization + continuation contract` closeout so this slice reuses the shared transcript harness rather than inventing its own.

items.note: Not landed on main. Four codexu/* worker attempts on 2026-04-23 (`a93551c9` / `a4c433f2` / `c9bc6d8d` / `a057f47c`) produced candidate Codex adapters but none merged — no `codex_*.go` file is tracked in `internal/hermes/` and `grep -i codex` over that package returns zero matches. TDD scope: upstream Codex is an OpenAI-Codex OAuth surface over the Responses API (not standard chat.completions), so the adapter must wrap `/v1/responses` at `https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex` — upstream surface lives at `agent/auxiliary_client.py:483 CodexAuxiliaryClient` / `:313 _CodexCompletionsAdapter` / `:501 _AsyncCodexCompletionsAdapter` / `:1038 _try_codex` (Responses→chat-completions shim, empty-output backfill from stream events, `gpt-5.2-codex` vs `gpt-5.3-codex` fallback), and the auth seam lives at `hermes_cli/auth.py:1451 refresh_codex_oauth_pure` / `:1546 _refresh_codex_auth_tokens` / `:1567 _import_codex_cli_tokens` / `:1601 resolve_codex_runtime_credentials` / `:3023 _codex_device_code_login`, with model enumeration at `hermes_cli/codex_models.py:147 get_codex_model_ids`. Pin behavior via fake-auth fixture tests: developer-message role handling, tool-call deltas, Responses→chat-completions envelope parity (including stream-event backfill when `response.output` arrives empty), `gpt-5.3-codex` rejection fallback, and explicit unsupported-feature errors. Upstream PR #12360 requires Codex auth state to live under Gormes's own home rather than touching `~/.codex/auth.json`, and upstream PR #2a2e5c0f requires 401/403 token-refresh failures to force a relogin rather than silently retrying with a stale token — freeze both behaviors as fake-auth unit tests before any live Codex request can fire. Land after `Provider interface + stream fixture harness`, `Tool-call normalization + continuation contract`, and `4.G Token vault` + `Multi-account auth` so the Codex adapter consumes a frozen credential seam rather than touching OS home paths directly.

not_ready_when: The slice reads or writes ~/.codex/auth.json as the primary state store.
blocked_by: Codex Responses assistant content role types
acceptance: Codex tokens persist under Gormes home with provider/account metadata.
401/403 refresh failures return relogin-required status and do not silently retry stale tokens.
Optional import from Codex CLI state is explicit and rejects expired tokens.
items.note: Keep Codex auth separate from Responses conversion. Upstream Hermes now stores OpenAI Codex auth in its own auth store and treats stale refresh failures as relogin-required; Gormes needs the same guarantee once 4.G token storage exists.
done_signal: Fake-auth fixtures prove Codex OAuth state is Gormes-owned and stale refresh failures require relogin.

items.name: Codex stream repair + tool-call leak sanitizer
items.contract: Codex Responses streams repair empty output and reject leaked function-call text before parent history is updated
items.degraded_mode: Provider logs explain repaired empty output, leaked tool-call text, and unsupported Codex stream items.
ready_when: Codex Responses conversion fixtures can replay streamed and non-streamed output without live credentials.
not_ready_when: Malformed tool calls are stored in assistant history as ordinary text or a repair path hides unsupported API features.
acceptance: Empty response.output with streamed output_text backfills final assistant content.
Leaked to=functions.* text is rejected or repaired before it reaches parent history.
Tool-call arguments are sanitized to valid JSON without inventing missing required values.
items.note: Complete: TDD added `internal/hermes/codex_stream_repair_test.go` and tightened the pure Codex Responses normalizer. Fixtures prove empty `response.output` with streamed `output_text` backfills final assistant content with repair diagnostics, leaked `to=functions.*` text is stripped and marked incomplete before parent history/event tokens are updated, descriptor-backed Codex function-call arguments are repaired to valid JSON, missing required arguments are rejected without invented values, and unsupported Responses output items fail loudly instead of being hidden by repair.
done_signal: Codex stream repair fixtures prove empty output, leaked tool-call text, and malformed arguments do not corrupt history.

items.name: Cross-provider reasoning-tag sanitization
items.note: TDD: port upstream PRs #ec48ec55 (strip `<think>` blocks from stored assistant content), #9489d157 (strip unterminated `<think>` blocks from visible content), and #bd01ec78 (strip all reasoning-tag variants from `/resume` recap) as one shared sanitization contract over `internal/hermes` event/assistant-content boundaries. Pure fixture-driven tests must cover closed `<think>...</think>` blocks, unterminated `<think>...` at stream EOF, and mixed reasoning-tag variants (`<think>`, `<reasoning>`, `<thought>`) without mutating the raw SSE transcript, and must separate (a) what is stored into assistant history, (b) what is rendered to operators, and (c) what `/resume`-style recap surfaces expose. Land before Codex/Gemini/OpenRouter/Bedrock bring new reasoning-model surfaces through the shared harness so each adapter inherits the same storage-vs-render behavior rather than reinventing it.

items.name: Tool-call argument repair + schema sanitizer
items.contract: Provider tool-call arguments are repaired or rejected against available tool schemas before execution
items.degraded_mode: Tool execution status reports schema-repair failures before a malformed provider call reaches the executor.
ready_when: Shared provider tool-call continuation fixtures are complete and tool descriptors expose required argument schemas.
not_ready_when: The slice silently invents missing required arguments or changes tool executor behavior instead of validating provider output.
unblocks: Codex stream repair + tool-call leak sanitizer
acceptance: Malformed JSON argument fragments from streamed tool calls are repaired only when the repair is deterministic.
Impossible repairs return a provider/tool-call error before execution.
Repair decisions use the current advertised tool schema so disabled or unavailable tools cannot be hallucinated into execution.
items.note: Complete: TDD added `internal/hermes/tool_call_argument_repair_test.go` and a shared provider-boundary repair/sanitizer. OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic streams now repair deterministic malformed JSON arguments against the advertised descriptor schema, reject impossible repairs, missing required fields, and unadvertised tools before EventDone reaches execution, and provider request mappers sanitize hostile schema shapes while preserving already-safe schemas. Verified with `go test ./internal/hermes ./internal/tools -count=1`.
done_signal: Tool-call repair fixtures prove malformed arguments are repaired or rejected before execution using the advertised schema.

drift_state.origin_decision: Hermes ad0ac894 widened DeepSeek/Kimi thinking-mode replay from assistant tool-call turns to all assistant turns, and the Gormes provider fixtures now validate the DeepSeek/Kimi, Azure OpenAI, Azure Anthropic, and Azure Foundry /models probe edges. Azure Foundry remains upstream-led: /models probing landed first, so the path-sniff row now reuses the existing AzureTransport constants before the combined transport read model can close. Later Hermes 192e7eb2 provider-rate drift is tracked under 4.H; b2d3308f/25ba6a4a doctor and gateway reasoning drift are tracked under 5.O/4.D.

items.name: Long session management
not_ready_when: The row is assigned as one implementation task instead of being split through context engine, token-budget, reference, and compression slices.

items.name: Context compression
items.note: Umbrella only: do not execute as one slice. Preserve cache-safe history semantics while the smaller compressor tasks land.

items.name: ContextEngine interface + status tool contract
items.contract: Stable context engine status and compression boundary
items.degraded_mode: Context status reports disabled compression, cooldowns, unknown tools, token-budget pressure, and replay gaps.
items.fixture: internal/hermes/testdata/context_status and internal/kernel context-engine replay fixtures
ready_when: Provider interface + stream fixture harness can replay context status without live provider calls.
not_ready_when: Compression is wired into the kernel before the context engine boundary and status contract are stable.
acceptance: Context status exposes window size, budget state, compression state, and unknown-tool errors.
Compression remains an explicit engine boundary, not a hidden kernel side effect.
Fixtures replay context status without live provider calls.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed a provider-owned Go ContextEngine contract in internal/hermes, a disabled engine with context_status payload fixtures for window, budget pressure, compression disabled/cooldown state, replay gaps, and structured unknown-context-tool errors. The kernel now snapshots context status, updates usage from provider EventDone, advertises context-engine tools, and dispatches them through the explicit engine boundary without calling Compress as a hidden side effect.
done_signal: Context status fixtures expose budget, compression state, and unknown-tool errors without live provider calls.

items.name: Compression token-budget trigger + summary sizing
items.contract: Go context compression budget state recalculates threshold, tail, and summary token budgets whenever the active model context window changes
items.degraded_mode: Context status reports compression disabled or unavailable instead of using stale budget values from a previous model window.
ready_when: ContextEngine interface + status tool contract is validated on main.
Provider-enforced context-length resolver is validated on main.
The implementation can be tested as pure internal/hermes budget logic with no live provider call.
not_ready_when: The slice mutates kernel message history, calls a summarizer LLM, ports context references, or changes provider routing instead of only implementing compressor budget/status behavior.
acceptance: Initializing the budget helper for a 200000-token context with threshold_percent=0.50 and summary_target_ratio=0.20 reports threshold_tokens=100000, tail_token_budget=20000, and max_summary_tokens=min(context_length*0.05, 12000).

Updating the active model context from 200000 tokens to 32000 tokens recalculates threshold_tokens, tail_token_budget, and max_summary_tokens from the new context length instead of preserving old budgets.
Summary target ratio is clamped to the upstream 0.10-0.80 range and the threshold floor preserves MINIMUM_CONTEXT_LENGTH-equivalent behavior.
The focused test mirrors Hermes TestUpdateModelBudgets without wiring compression into the kernel or requiring provider credentials.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed a pure internal/hermes ContextCompressorBudget helper mirroring upstream Hermes 5401a008 budget recalculation. Fixtures prove 200K initialization, 200K to 32K model-window updates, summary_target_ratio clamping, the 64K minimum-context threshold floor, max_summary_tokens sizing, and unavailable degraded status for unknown model windows without kernel wiring, provider credentials, summarizer calls, or message-history mutation.
done_signal: internal/hermes/context_compressor_budget_test.go proves initialization and UpdateModelContext-style model switches recalculate threshold, tail, and max-summary budgets from the active context window.

provenance.note: Hermes defines the compressor budget behavior; Gormes must adapt it to Go-native ContextEngine budget/status code without importing Python runtime.

items.name: Aux compression headroom for system and tool schemas
items.contract: Compression feasibility lowers the raw-message threshold below the auxiliary model context window by reserving deterministic system-prompt, tool-schema, flush-instruction, and safety headroom
items.degraded_mode: Context status reports headroom-clamped compression thresholds instead of allowing the first compression or flush-memory request to overflow the auxiliary model.
ready_when: Compression token-budget trigger + summary sizing is validated on main.
Provider-enforced context-length resolver is validated on main.
The test can estimate tool-schema overhead from local descriptors or synthetic ToolDescriptor fixtures without opening a provider connection.
not_ready_when: The slice mutates message history, calls a summarizer, changes model routing, or subtracts a hard-coded 12000 tokens without accounting for tool descriptors.
acceptance: When main-model threshold exceeds a 128000-token auxiliary context, the budget helper subtracts estimate_request_tokens_rough-equivalent tool overhead plus 12000 system/flush/safety tokens before setting threshold_tokens.
With 50 synthetic tool schemas, threshold_tokens is strictly below the auxiliary context and remains above minimumContextCompressorContextLength.
When overhead would drop below the 64000-token floor, threshold_tokens clamps to the floor and reports headroom_clamped evidence.
The focused test mirrors Hermes test_auto_lowered_threshold_reserves_headroom_for_tools_and_system and test_headroom_floors_at_minimum_context without provider credentials or kernel history mutation.
items.note: Complete: internal/hermes/context_compressor_headroom_test.go covers the auxiliary-bound threshold path with synthetic tool descriptors and the minimum-floor path. ContextCompressorBudget now estimates provider-visible tool descriptor overhead, reserves that plus 12000 fixed system/flush/safety tokens, lowers threshold_tokens below the auxiliary context when needed, and reports headroom_clamped evidence without provider calls or history mutation.
done_signal: Headroom fixtures prove tool-schema overhead plus 12000 reserved tokens lower auxiliary-bound thresholds and clamp at the 64000-token floor without provider calls.

provenance.note: Hermes defines the auxiliary compression feasibility fix; Gormes should adapt it to Go-native ContextCompressorBudget/status logic.

items.name: Aux compression provider-aware context cap
items.contract: Auxiliary compression feasibility resolves the auxiliary model context window with the active provider identity before applying headroom and threshold budgets
items.degraded_mode: Context status reports provider-cap source, raw-model fallback, or missing auxiliary context evidence instead of budgeting compression against the wrong raw OpenAI-compatible window.
ready_when: Provider-enforced context-length resolver is validated on main.
Aux compression headroom for system and tool schemas is validated on main.
The slice can use fake ModelContextResolver inputs for openai-codex gpt-5.5 raw 1050000 tokens vs provider cap 272000 tokens without live provider calls.
not_ready_when: The slice calls a summarizer, mutates message history, opens a network provider, or only repeats generic context resolver tests without proving the auxiliary compression feasibility path forwards provider identity.
blocked_by: Aux compression headroom for system and tool schemas
acceptance: A fake auxiliary feasibility check for provider=openai-codex model=gpt-5.5 raw_context=1050000 resolves auxiliary_context_length=272000 with source=provider_cap before threshold/headroom math runs.
The same model with provider=openai or an empty provider does not borrow the openai-codex cap and reports raw-model fallback or unavailable evidence according to the resolver fixture.
ContextCompressorBudget receives the provider-resolved AuxiliaryContextLength and lowers threshold_tokens below 272000 after tool-schema plus 12000 fixed headroom.
Status evidence exposes the context source or lookup error so operators can distinguish provider-cap budgeting from models.dev fallback.
items.note: Complete: internal/hermes/context_compressor_provider_cap_test.go proves the auxiliary compression budget path forwards provider identity into ModelContextResolver before headroom math. The fixture uses fake resolver inputs for openai-codex/gpt-5.5 raw 1050000 versus provider cap 272000, confirms openai and missing-provider paths do not borrow the Codex cap, and exposes auxiliary_context_source / auxiliary_context_lookup_error evidence while ContextCompressorBudget lowers threshold_tokens below the provider cap after tool-schema plus 12000 fixed headroom.
done_signal: Provider-cap compression fixtures prove the auxiliary feasibility path forwards provider identity, uses the 272000 Codex cap, and reports fallback/source evidence without live provider calls.

provenance.note: Hermes defines the provider-forwarding regression; Gormes should adapt it to the Go ContextCompressorBudget and ModelContextResolver boundary.

items.name: Tool-result pruning + protected head/tail summary
items.note: TDD: freeze old tool-output pruning, protected system/first-turn head messages, recent-tail preservation, and summary prefix compatibility before mutating live message history. Must keep tool-call arguments JSON valid when shrinking per upstream PR #3128d9fc — a pruning pass that truncates an argument string mid-JSON breaks the continuation payload on the next turn, so the head/tail contract has to respect tool-call argument boundaries and never emit partial JSON values.

items.name: Aux compression single-prompt threshold reconciliation
items.contract: Auxiliary compression budgeting follows Hermes 5006b220 by treating the summarizer request as raw messages plus one small user instruction, not as a system-prompt-plus-tool-schema memory-flush request
items.degraded_mode: Context status reports whether a threshold used legacy_headroom, single_prompt_aux, provider_cap, or unavailable evidence so operators can see why compression starts early or late.
ready_when: Compression token-budget trigger + summary sizing and Aux compression provider-aware context cap are validated on main.
The row can be proven with pure ContextCompressorBudget fixtures and synthetic tool descriptors; no summarizer, provider call, or kernel history mutation is required.
not_ready_when: The slice ports history pruning, manual compression feedback, context references, provider routing, or Goncho memory extraction instead of only reconciling auxiliary threshold math/status.
acceptance: A 200000-token main window with a 128000-token auxiliary context and 50 synthetic tool schemas sets threshold_tokens to the auxiliary context, not aux minus tool schema plus 12000 fixed headroom.
The budget status no longer reports request_headroom_tokens or headroom_clamped for compression-only auxiliary calls, but preserves provider_cap evidence from the resolver.
A compatibility fixture documents the removed legacy headroom behavior and fails if flush_memories assumptions are reintroduced into compression budgeting.
Existing provider-cap and compressor-budget tests remain green after the threshold/status expectation update.
items.note: Complete: TDD added internal/hermes/context_compressor_single_prompt_test.go and updated the prior headroom/provider-cap fixtures for Hermes 5006b220 semantics. ContextCompressorBudget now sets auxiliary-bound compression thresholds to aux_context directly, reports threshold_source evidence for single_prompt_aux, provider_cap, main_context, or unavailable, preserves auxiliary_context_source provider_cap evidence, and omits request_headroom_tokens/headroom_clamped from compression-only status. Compatibility fixtures fail if tool-schema plus 12000 flush-memory headroom is reintroduced.
done_signal: Single-prompt compression fixtures prove auxiliary-bound thresholds use aux_context directly, preserve provider-cap evidence, and no longer reserve tool-schema or flush-memory headroom.

provenance.note: Hermes removed the memory-flush caller that justified tool-schema/system headroom; Gormes needs a Go-native budget/status update while keeping provider-cap resolution.

Gormes has the Go ContextEngine status boundary, provider-cap resolver, and legacy headroom helper, so this subphase remains upstream-led until the single-prompt threshold reconciliation, tool-result pruning, protected head/tail summaries, and manual compression feedback/context-reference slices land.

items.name: [SYSTEM:→[IMPORTANT: meta-instruction prefix rename for Azure content filter compatibility
items.contract: Single rename of the bracketed meta-instruction prefix used for skill-invocation and cron-heartbeat prompts: change every occurrence of the literal string "[SYSTEM:" to "[IMPORTANT:" in (a) internal/skills/commands.go (currently at line 94 inside the BuildSkill block string template) and (b) internal/cron/heartbeat.go's CronHeartbeatPrefix constant. The replacement is byte-for-byte over a 7→10 character literal; semantic meaning to the model is unchanged. Update the two existing assertions in internal/skills/preprocessing_commands_test.go (string literal `[SYSTEM: The user has invoked`) and the two assertions in internal/cron/heartbeat_test.go (HasPrefix and contained "[SYSTEM:") to match the new prefix. No public Go API renames, no struct shape changes, no provider/transport edits, no schema migrations.
items.degraded_mode: Cron-heartbeat and skill-invocation prompts continue to assemble in the same shape; only the leading bracketed marker token differs. Azure OpenAI content-filter (Default/DefaultV2) no longer rejects with HTTP 400 'prompt-injection attempt' when these markers appear at message head.
ready_when: Both internal/skills/commands.go and internal/cron/heartbeat.go currently use the literal "[SYSTEM:" prefix (verified 2026-04-26 by grep).
Both *_test.go files assert against the literal "[SYSTEM:" prefix and will need synchronized updates inside this slice's write_scope.
No upstream gate beyond the upstream Hermes commit d7a34682 already merged.
not_ready_when: The slice changes the body content of the cron heartbeat or skill-invocation prompts — only the bracketed prefix token rename is in scope.
The slice introduces a runtime feature flag, environment variable, or config knob to switch between [SYSTEM: and [IMPORTANT: — the rename is unconditional.
The slice touches gateway prompt assembly, provider transports, or any runtime that does not currently emit the [SYSTEM: marker.
acceptance: TestCronHeartbeatBuildPromptUsesImportantPrefix asserts BuildPrompt output starts with "[IMPORTANT:" and contains the existing scheduled-cron-job description body unchanged.
TestCronHeartbeatPrefixConstantValue asserts CronHeartbeatPrefix is exactly the new "[IMPORTANT: You are running as a scheduled cron job. " prefix.
TestPreprocessingCommandsRendersImportantMarker (updated existing test) asserts the rendered skill block contains "[IMPORTANT: The user has invoked" and no longer contains "[SYSTEM:" anywhere.
TestSkillsCommandsBuildBlockNeverEmitsLegacyMarker (new) renders BuildSkillBlock against a synthetic active skill and asserts strings.Contains(out, "[SYSTEM:") == false.
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass with no compile errors after the rename.
items.note: Complete: cron heartbeat and skill-invocation prompts now emit [IMPORTANT: while preserving the existing prompt bodies and public Go APIs. Fixtures assert the new prefix, the absence of legacy [SYSTEM: markers from the relevant rendered prompts, and the exact heartbeat constant body. Mirrors upstream Hermes d7a34682 (fix(prompts): replace [SYSTEM: with [IMPORTANT: to avoid Azure content filter, fixes #6576).
done_signal: internal/cron/heartbeat_test.go and internal/skills/preprocessing_commands_test.go assert the [IMPORTANT: prefix and the codebase contains zero remaining "[SYSTEM:" string literals under internal/skills and internal/cron.

provenance.upstream_ref: hermes:d7a34682 fix(prompts): replace [SYSTEM: with [IMPORTANT: to avoid Azure content filter
provenance.note: Direct port of the upstream Azure-content-filter compatibility rename. Gormes uses [SYSTEM: in two narrow spots; this slice closes the drift and unblocks Azure OpenAI traffic without touching prompt body content.

not_ready_when: The slice implements routing/fallback policy or pulls live models.dev/network data during unit tests.

Provider-specific caps for Codex OAuth, Copilot, and Nous win over model-info fallbacks when present.

The pure resolver has no live provider credentials, disk cache, routing policy, or network dependency.

items.name: Model pricing/capability registry fixtures

The registry is static in internal/hermes/model_registry.go and performs no live models.dev access or routing decisions.
