items.name: CLI deterministic tip selector
items.contract: internal/cli adds a fixed exported corpus `Tips []string` of at least 30 non-empty entries (each unique, none containing newlines) and a pure function `TipFor(seed int64) string` that returns Tips[(seed mod len(Tips))]; tip text is owned by Gormes and does not need to mirror upstream tip strings
items.degraded_mode: Callers that want a non-deterministic random tip can inject `time.Now().UnixNano()` as the seed; tests always inject a fixed seed so output is deterministic.
ready_when: internal/cli already exposes pure helpers (banner.go, output.go); adding a single new file with a corpus + selector compiles cleanly.
This slice does not need any random source, clock, network, filesystem, or upstream tip-text parity.
not_ready_when: The slice imports math/rand without an injected seed, reads files, registers commands, or copies upstream tip text verbatim (which would couple Gormes to Hermes string churn).
The slice spans dump.py or webhook.py — those land in their own rows.
acceptance: TestTips_CorpusInvariants: len(Tips) >= 30; every entry is non-empty, contains no '\n', and the corpus has no duplicate entries.
TestTipFor_DeterministicForFixedSeed: TipFor(42) == TipFor(42) across two calls and equals Tips[42 mod len(Tips)].
TestTipFor_HandlesNegativeSeed: TipFor(-1) returns a non-empty string from Tips and never panics (the function normalizes negative seeds before indexing).
TestTipFor_HandlesZeroSeed: TipFor(0) == Tips[0].
TestTipFor_DistributesAcrossCorpus: calling TipFor(int64(i)) for i in [0, len(Tips)) returns each Tips entry exactly once.
items.note: Reshape of the prior quarantined 5.O row. The previous contract bundled tips + dump + webhook in one slice with a 'tips_dump_webhook_test.go' fixture name — three concept layers that workers couldn't land atomically. This row narrows scope to one file with one corpus + one selector and five named tests. Dump and webhook are tracked as separate sibling rows below. Gormes owns the tip text; we do not port the Hermes corpus verbatim because tip strings reference hermes-specific commands (/save, /personality, hermes -c) that do not all exist in Gormes yet. The split intentionally drops the prior health/quarantine block; the contract is materially replaced.

items.name: CLI webhook URL normalizer
items.contract: internal/cli adds a pure function `NormalizeWebhookURL(raw string) (string, error)` that trims whitespace, requires scheme http or https, strips trailing slashes from the path, lowercases the host, rejects userinfo (raw URLs containing '@' before the host) with a sentinel error, and returns the canonicalized URL string; no network call is made
items.degraded_mode: Callers handle the typed error classes (ErrWebhookURLEmpty, ErrWebhookURLBadScheme, ErrWebhookURLUserInfoForbidden, ErrWebhookURLParseFailed) instead of treating any non-empty string as a valid webhook target.
ready_when: CLI deterministic tip selector lands so internal/cli has a stable surface; this row only adds one pure function with sentinel errors.
not_ready_when: The slice opens network sockets, ports webhook signing, registers a Cobra command, or modifies any other internal/cli file.
unblocks: CLI command registry parity + active-turn busy policy
acceptance: TestNormalizeWebhookURL_TrimsAndCanonicalizes: NormalizeWebhookURL('  HTTPS://Example.COM/Hooks/x/  ') returns ('https://example.com/Hooks/x', nil).
TestNormalizeWebhookURL_StripsTrailingSlashOnRoot: NormalizeWebhookURL('https://example.com/') returns ('https://example.com', nil).
TestNormalizeWebhookURL_PreservesQueryAndFragment: NormalizeWebhookURL('https://example.com/x/?a=1#f') returns ('https://example.com/x?a=1#f', nil).
TestNormalizeWebhookURL_RejectsEmpty: NormalizeWebhookURL('   ') returns ('', ErrWebhookURLEmpty).
TestNormalizeWebhookURL_RejectsNonHTTP: NormalizeWebhookURL('ftp://example.com') and NormalizeWebhookURL('javascript:alert(1)') each return ('', ErrWebhookURLBadScheme).
TestNormalizeWebhookURL_RejectsUserInfo: NormalizeWebhookURL('https://user:pass@example.com/x') returns ('', ErrWebhookURLUserInfoForbidden) — credentials must not be embedded in webhook URLs.
items.note: Sibling slice after deterministic tip selector. Keeps the webhook URL contract at one pure function with six named tests so workers can land it atomically without porting webhook signing or command wiring.
done_signal: internal/cli/webhook.go declares NormalizeWebhookURL plus the four sentinel errors; six named tests pass; no other internal/cli file is modified.

provenance.note: Hermes accepts arbitrary webhook URLs; Gormes lands a stricter normalizer first so command handlers can rely on canonical input.

items.name: Gateway /reasoning command parser
items.contract: Pure parser internal/gateway/reasoning_command.go exposes type ReasoningAction int (ReasoningActionShow, ReasoningActionSet, ReasoningActionReset), type ReasoningEffort string (high|low|medium|""), type ReasoningCommand struct{Action ReasoningAction; Effort ReasoningEffort; Global bool}, and ParseReasoningCommand(args []string) (ReasoningCommand, error). Empty args returns Action=Show. "high|low|medium" returns Action=Set with that Effort. Trailing "--global" sets Global=true. "reset" alone returns Action=Reset, Global=false. "reset --global" returns an error matching ErrResetGlobalUnsupported. Unknown effort returns ErrInvalidEffort. No state, no clock, no I/O.
items.degraded_mode: Parser surfaces typed errors (ErrInvalidEffort, ErrResetGlobalUnsupported) so the dispatcher can render the upstream warning class without re-parsing.
ready_when: internal/gateway already compiles and has commands.go; this row adds a sibling reasoning_command.go and a _test.go.
Pure-function table tests with synthetic []string args are sufficient.
not_ready_when: The row touches manager.go, persists config, or wires gateway dispatch — those land in the follow-up apply/dispatch row.
acceptance: TestParseReasoningCommand_ShowFormReturnsActionShow ([]) returns Action=Show.
TestParseReasoningCommand_SetSessionScoped (["high"]) returns Action=Set, Effort=high, Global=false.
TestParseReasoningCommand_SetGlobal (["low","--global"]) returns Action=Set, Effort=low, Global=true.
TestParseReasoningCommand_ResetSession (["reset"]) returns Action=Reset, Global=false, no error.
TestParseReasoningCommand_RejectGlobalReset (["reset","--global"]) returns ErrResetGlobalUnsupported.
TestParseReasoningCommand_RejectInvalidEffort (["bogus"]) returns ErrInvalidEffort.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed pure parser `internal/gateway/reasoning_command.go` and table tests in `internal/gateway/reasoning_command_test.go`. ParseReasoningCommand recognizes the show form on empty args, the set form for high|low|medium, the trailing --global modifier, and the reset form (session-scoped only). Unknown effort tokens return ErrInvalidEffort and `reset --global` returns ErrResetGlobalUnsupported, both wrapped via fmt.Errorf so dispatchers can errors.Is them without re-parsing. The parser stays pure: no manager.go, no config persistence, no gateway dispatch — the apply + dispatch slice consumes these typed errors next.
done_signal: internal/gateway/reasoning_command_test.go fixtures prove parser correctness across show/set/global/reset/invalid forms.

items.name: Gateway /reasoning apply + dispatch
items.contract: internal/gateway/reasoning_command.go also exposes type SessionReasoningState struct{Effort ReasoningEffort; Source string} where Source is "session"|"global"|"unset", type ReasoningReply struct{Effort ReasoningEffort; Scope string; PersistFailed bool}, and ApplyReasoningCommand(state SessionReasoningState, cmd ReasoningCommand, persistGlobal func(ReasoningEffort) error) (SessionReasoningState, ReasoningReply). Action=Show returns current state unchanged. Action=Set with Global=false mutates session-only. Action=Set with Global=true calls persistGlobal; on failure falls back to session-only and sets PersistFailed=true. Action=Reset clears the session override. Internal/gateway/manager.go wires "/reasoning" through the existing command registry to ParseReasoningCommand + ApplyReasoningCommand for the calling session.
items.degraded_mode: ReasoningReply.PersistFailed surfaces a global-save failure without changing session state; Source distinguishes session vs global vs unset.
ready_when: Gateway /reasoning command parser is fixture_ready or validated.
internal/gateway/manager.go already has a command-dispatch seam this slice can extend without a registry refactor.
not_ready_when: The slice writes a model picker, /new behavior, or broad command-registry parity.
The slice persists reasoning changes outside the persistGlobal callback or mutates other sessions.
unblocks: CLI command registry parity + active-turn busy policy
acceptance: TestApplyReasoningCommand_ShowReturnsCurrentScope returns ReasoningReply containing Effort and Scope from the injected state.
TestApplyReasoningCommand_SetSessionMutatesOnly leaves global persistence alone.
TestApplyReasoningCommand_SetGlobalCallsPersistGlobal calls the callback with the requested Effort.
TestApplyReasoningCommand_GlobalPersistFallback when persistGlobal returns an error, sets PersistFailed=true and falls back to session state.
TestApplyReasoningCommand_ResetClearsSessionState sets Source="unset" and Effort="".
TestGatewayManagerDispatchesReasoning verifies that the existing command registry routes "/reasoning" through Parse + Apply for the calling session only.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed `SessionReasoningState`, `ReasoningReply`, and `ApplyReasoningCommand` in `internal/gateway/reasoning_command.go` with `Source`/`Scope` tags `unset|session|global`. Show returns the injected state unchanged; session-only Set mutates without invoking persistGlobal; global Set calls persistGlobal once with the requested effort and pins Source=global on success; persistGlobal failure falls back to session-only state with PersistFailed=true; Reset clears Source to unset and Effort to "". `internal/gateway/manager.go` adds `ManagerConfig.PersistReasoningGlobal` plus `Manager.DispatchReasoning(sessionKey, args)` keyed by chat key, so per-session reasoning state is isolated and a missing callback degrades to the session-only fallback path. `TestGatewayManagerDispatchesReasoning` covers session-only set, show, cross-session isolation, --global persist, post-global isolation, reset, and invalid-effort error propagation; `TestGatewayManagerDispatchReasoningGlobalPersistFallback` proves a callback error becomes PersistFailed without surfacing the error to the caller.
done_signal: internal/gateway/reasoning_command_test.go covers session-only set, global persist callback, persistGlobal fallback, and reset; manager test proves dispatch.

provenance.note: Apply + dispatch slice; depends on the parser slice landing first.

ready_when: CLI profile name validator and CLI profile root resolver are validated, exporting ValidateProfileName/ResolveProfileRoot.
not_ready_when: The slice mutates process env, creates wrapper scripts, copies profile directories, registers a Cobra command, or modifies internal/config.

acceptance: TestReadActiveProfile_UnsetWhenMissing: ReadActiveProfile(<nonexistent path>) returns ('', ErrActiveProfileUnset).
TestWriteActiveProfile_RoundTrip: WriteActiveProfile then ReadActiveProfile returns the same name with nil error.
TestWriteActiveProfile_RejectsInvalidName: WriteActiveProfile(path, 'Bad Name') returns ErrProfileNameInvalidChars and leaves the file unchanged.

TestWriteActiveProfile_AtomicTempRename: a fixture that pre-creates a sentinel file at path+'.tmp' confirms WriteActiveProfile completes successfully (proving temp-file + rename) and the final file content equals the new name byte-for-byte.
TestClearActiveProfile_IdempotentMissing: ClearActiveProfile(<nonexistent path>) returns nil.
TestClearActiveProfile_RemovesExisting: after WriteActiveProfile, ClearActiveProfile removes the file and the next ReadActiveProfile returns ErrActiveProfileUnset.
items.note: Sibling row after validator and root resolver. Persists only the active-profile name through atomic temp-file + rename so workers can land the store boundary without hand-rolling concurrency.
done_signal: internal/cli/active_profile.go declares ReadActiveProfile/WriteActiveProfile/ClearActiveProfile and ErrActiveProfileUnset; six named tests pass; no other internal/cli, internal/config, or cmd/gormes file is modified.

provenance.note: Hermes persists active_profile as a plain text file under HERMES_HOME; Gormes does the same with atomic write semantics under the resolved XDG profile root.

items.name: Top-level oneshot flag and model/provider resolver
items.contract: Gormes accepts a top-level `-z/--oneshot` prompt plus `--model`, `--provider`, GORMES_INFERENCE_MODEL, and GORMES_INFERENCE_PROVIDER overrides with Hermes-compatible ambiguity errors before any agent execution starts
items.degraded_mode: CLI parse/status output returns exit code 2 and an actionable stderr error when --provider is set without an explicit model, rather than silently pairing it with a stale configured model.
ready_when: The Cobra root command can parse top-level flags without starting the TUI, api_server health check, or gateway transports.
The first slice owns only argument/env/config resolution and does not need to execute a model turn.
not_ready_when: The slice opens the TUI, starts the kernel, sends provider requests, changes config file schema broadly, or implements stdout capture and tool approval behavior in the same change.
acceptance: `gormes -z 'hi' --model fixture-model` parses without invoking runTUI or api_server health checks.
`gormes -z 'hi' --provider openrouter` exits 2 unless --model or GORMES_INFERENCE_MODEL is present.
Environment model/provider overrides are read only for oneshot and do not mutate persisted config.
The resolver records whether the model came from flag, env, or config and whether provider auto-detection is required for the execution slice.
items.note: Hermes 7c8c031f/a9fa73a6 added `hermes -z` plus top-level --model/--provider and env fallback behavior. Gormes needs the parser/resolver contract first so execution workers do not mix flag semantics with stdout-only turn running.
done_signal: Oneshot flag fixtures prove top-level parsing, provider-without-model exit 2, env fallback, and no TUI/gateway startup during resolution.

provenance.note: Hermes defines the oneshot CLI contract; Gormes must adapt it to Cobra/config/model-routing without Python CLI imports.

items.name: Oneshot final-output writer boundary

items.degraded_mode: CLI output reports provider/client setup failures on stderr with nonzero exit codes while banners, render frames, tool previews, logs, and session IDs never enter stdout.

not_ready_when: The slice implements interactive TUI behavior, starts gateway transports, changes tool registry defaults, or decides noninteractive dangerous-command approval policy.

acceptance: A fake-client `gormes -z 'hi' --model fixture-model` command prints exactly the final assistant content plus one trailing newline to stdout.
Status frames, tool-progress frames, banners, session IDs, and logs are absent from stdout and either suppressed or routed to stderr under explicit fixtures.
Provider/client setup failures return nonzero exit status with operator-readable stderr and empty stdout.
The command starts no TUI, gateway transport, api_server bridge, or live provider request in the focused fixture.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed cmd/gormes/oneshot_output_test.go and the native one-shot runner boundary. Fixtures inject a fake hermes.Client, prove one kernel ChatRequest for `gormes -z 'hi' --model fixture-model`, exact stdout final content plus one newline, setup failures on stderr with exit 1, and no TUI/gateway/api_server startup or stdout leakage of render/status/session/tool/log text. Safety/clarify behavior follows in the dependent row.
done_signal: Oneshot output fixtures prove one fake kernel turn, stdout-only final content, stderr-only setup failures, and no TUI/gateway/provider startup.

provenance.note: Hermes defines stdout-only one-shot behavior; Gormes owns the native kernel/output-capture adaptation.

items.name: Platform toolset config persistence + MCP sentinel

Numeric YAML keys and list entries are normalized to strings so sorting and persistence cannot panic.
A no_mcp sentinel suppresses MCP servers only for the configured platform and is not exposed as a runtime toolset.
Restricted toolsets such as discord and discord_admin cannot be saved for non-Discord platforms.
items.note: Complete: TDD added internal/cli platform toolset config helpers without command wiring. Fixtures prove platform-default supersets such as hermes-cli/hermes-telegram are stripped while unknown MCP server names survive, YAML-shaped numeric keys/list entries normalize to strings before sorting/persistence, no_mcp suppresses MCP only for its platform without becoming a runtime toolset, and manifest-backed Discord restrictions prevent discord/discord_admin from being saved outside the Discord platform.
done_signal: Toolset config fixtures prove platform-default stripping, MCP preservation/no_mcp, numeric normalization, and Discord platform restrictions.

provenance.note: Hermes defines the persistence edge cases; Gormes should keep the first port as pure config logic before command UI wiring.

Built-in picker rows come from the upstream parity manifest, plugin-declared toolsets append only for unseen keys, bundled Spotify duplicates collapse with built-in label/description/source preserved, unique plugin toolsets remain disabled metadata rows, and duplicate evidence is reported without executing plugin runtime code.

items.name: Service restart active-status poller

Fixtures distinguish active-after-delay, active-after-RestartSec, timeout, missing service manager, malformed RestartUSec fallback, and crashed-after-restart outcomes with operator-readable evidence.

items.note: Complete: TDD added a pure internal/cli active-status poller with injected fake runner and fake clock. items.name: CLI log redactor for known secret shapes
items.contract: internal/cli/log_redact.go exposes RedactLine(line []byte) ([]byte, int) where the int is the number of redactions applied. Matches and replaces with "[REDACTED]": (1) Bearer XXX in any header line, (2) api_key=VALUE or x-api-key: VALUE, (3) Telegram bot tokens NN:XXXXXXXX (digits + colon + >=20 alnum/_/-), (4) Slack xoxb-/xoxp-/xoxs- tokens, (5) OpenAI sk-* keys longer than 16 chars. Returns input unchanged with count=0 if no match. Pure: only regexp + bytes packages from stdlib.
items.degraded_mode: Redactor counts replacements per line so the snapshot caller can attach a per-section Redacted field without re-scanning.
ready_when: internal/cli already compiles; this row adds a sibling log_redact.go + _test.go.
Tests use fixed []byte literals — no file I/O.
not_ready_when: The slice reads files, walks XDG paths, or uploads anywhere.
The slice adds new secret shapes beyond the five listed.
unblocks: CLI log snapshot reader using shared redactor
acceptance: TestRedactLine_BearerToken returns redacted line and count=1 for "Authorization: Bearer abc123def456".
TestRedactLine_ApiKeyEqualsValue covers "api_key=sk-prod-XYZ" and "x-api-key: sk-test-...".
TestRedactLine_TelegramBotToken redacts "12345:ABCDEFGHabcdefgh1234567890".
TestRedactLine_SlackTokens covers xoxb-, xoxp-, xoxs- tokens.
TestRedactLine_OpenAIStyleKey redacts sk-* longer than 16 chars only.
TestRedactLine_NoMatchPreservesInput returns input unchanged with count=0.
items.note: Split off from the larger log snapshot row. Pure regex + bytes; six table tests in one cycle. The follow-up snapshot reader will import this file and apply RedactLine per line.

items.name: CLI log snapshot reader using shared redactor
items.contract: internal/cli/log_snapshot.go exposes type LogClass string (LogClassMain, LogClassToolAudit), type LogSnapshotRoots struct{LogPath, ToolAuditPath string}, type SnapshotOpts struct{HeadBytes, TailBytes int64} (defaults 64 KiB + 16 KiB), type LogSection struct{Class LogClass; Path string; Head, Tail []byte; Missing, Truncated bool; Redacted int; Unreadable string} and type Snapshot struct{Sections []LogSection}. SnapshotLogs(roots LogSnapshotRoots, opts SnapshotOpts) Snapshot reads each log path, truncates head+tail when the file exceeds HeadBytes+TailBytes, and runs RedactLine over each line, summing the count into LogSection.Redacted. Missing files set Missing=true; permission/read errors set Unreadable to the io error message; the function never returns an error and never panics.
items.degraded_mode: Per-class results carry Missing, Truncated, Redacted, Unreadable so doctor/status can render evidence without failing.
ready_when: CLI log redactor for known secret shapes is fixture_ready or validated so this slice can import RedactLine.
Tests use t.TempDir() and inject LogSnapshotRoots — no XDG resolution inside this helper.
not_ready_when: The slice resolves XDG paths or reads config.LogPath() inside the helper.
The slice adds gateway/error log file constants — those rows land once those files start being written.
The slice uploads to paste.rs/dpaste, creates archives, or changes doctor exit codes.
blocked_by: CLI log redactor for known secret shapes
unblocks: CLI status summary over native stores
acceptance: TestSnapshotLogs_MissingFileSetsMissingTrue records LogSection{Missing: true} without an error.
TestSnapshotLogs_HeadAndTailTruncation produces Head and Tail with sizes equal to opts.HeadBytes and opts.TailBytes when the file is larger than their sum.
TestSnapshotLogs_AppliesRedactorPerLine sums Redacted across head and tail lines.
TestSnapshotLogs_UnreadableFileSetsUnreadableField records the io error message and continues with the other LogClass.
TestSnapshotLogs_BothFilesMissingReturnsTwoSections includes both LogClassMain and LogClassToolAudit even when both files are absent.
items.note: Reader slice. Imports RedactLine from the redactor row. Caller resolves config.LogPath() and config.ToolAuditLogPath() and passes them in — keeps the helper testable with t.TempDir() only.
done_signal: internal/cli/log_snapshot_test.go fixtures prove Missing/Truncated/Redacted/Unreadable evidence under t.TempDir() injection.

provenance.note: Reader slice; depends on the redactor slice landing first.

drift_state.origin_decision: Hermes 25ba6a4a moved /reasoning to session-scoped-by-default gateway state with --global persistence; b2d3308f fixed doctor so bare custom provider config is accepted; and 2c56dce0/1fdc31b2/8bbeaea6 fixed custom-provider model switching so named providers, configured cloud aliases, and env-template secrets survive CLI/TUI model changes. Gormes has native oneshot/model override and helper rows, but it still lacks typed reasoning-effort propagation, gateway /reasoning session overrides, custom-endpoint doctor readiness fixtures, and a pure custom-provider model-switch resolver, so this CLI/gateway surface remains upstream-led.

items.name: Unix installer (install.sh) source-backed update flow
items.note: TDD slice 1 of installer parity (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-23-gormes-installer-parity.md`): port upstream Unix installer semantics into canonical `scripts/install.sh`, with the site embedding a synced `www.gormes.ai/internal/site/installers/install.sh` copy. The installer uses user-scoped PATH publication, a managed checkout under a Hermes-analogy install home, rerun-as-update behavior, and no root-owned target paths. Cover path selection, managed checkout, update-on-rerun, and publish+verify flow via a new `install_unix_test.go` Go test that executes the script under a temporary fake toolchain — no network calls in unit tests. Do not install Hermes/Python/Node: Gormes' current feature set does not require them.

If Gormes adopts the FHS root default, legacy installs under $GORMES_INSTALL_HOME/gormes-agent are preserved in place and site copy stays byte-equal to scripts/install.sh.
No test performs a live git clone, package-manager install, or root-owned write.

items.name: TUI gateway tool-progress mode normalizer
items.contract: Pure helper internal/tuigateway/tool_progress_mode.go exposes NormalizeToolProgressMode(raw any) string returning one of {off, new, all, verbose}. Mirrors upstream tui_gateway/server.py:_load_tool_progress_mode (line 661). Boolean false → off, boolean true → all, string is lowercased and trimmed; any value outside the recognised set defaults to all. No config file reads, no session state.
items.degraded_mode: TUI gateway falls back to all-progress until this normalizer is fixture-backed.
ready_when: Pure helper only — table-driven tests with synthetic raw values are sufficient. No transport wiring, no session map, no config file read in this slice.
internal/tuigateway/ does not yet exist; this row creates the package with one helper file and one test file.
not_ready_when: The slice creates NewRemoteClient, registers an http handler, opens a session map, or reads from the live config file.
The slice ports completion-path or tool-summary helpers in the same change.
acceptance: TestNormalizeToolProgressMode_RecognizedValues maps off/new/all/verbose verbatim.
TestNormalizeToolProgressMode_BooleanFalseToOff and BooleanTrueToAll handle Python-style truthy values arriving as Go bool.
TestNormalizeToolProgressMode_LowercasesAndTrims accepts ` ALL ` and `New`.
TestNormalizeToolProgressMode_UnknownDefaultsToAll for `loud`, empty string, nil.
Helper has no side effects; package internal/tuigateway exists after this row lands.
items.note: Complete: internal/tuigateway package created with NormalizeToolProgressMode(raw any) string. Bool false→off, bool true→all, recognised strings round-trip after trim+lowercase, unknown values (including nil, numbers, slices, empty/whitespace-only strings) collapse to all. Pure helper — no config reads, no session state, no transport. Verified by table-driven tests in internal/tuigateway/tool_progress_mode_test.go covering RecognizedValues, BooleanFalseToOff, BooleanTrueToAll, LowercasesAndTrims, UnknownDefaultsToAll.
done_signal: internal/tuigateway/tool_progress_mode_test.go fixtures prove off/new/all/verbose normalization including bool and unknown-defaults paths.

provenance.note: Hermes defines the four-mode invariant; Gormes ports it as a pure Go helper before transport.

items.name: TUI gateway completion path normalizer
items.contract: Pure helper internal/tuigateway/completion_path.go exposes NormalizeCompletionPath(in string, isWindows bool) string. Mirrors upstream tui_gateway/server.py:_normalize_completion_path (line 428): expands a leading ~ to the operator's home dir (caller injects the home value via a small stubbable hook), and on non-Windows callers also rewrites Windows-style C:/foo into /mnt/c/foo. No filesystem stat, no os.UserHomeDir inside the helper — caller threads a HomeFn or homeDir string.
items.degraded_mode: Remote-TUI completion menus echo the raw path until this normalizer is fixture-backed.
ready_when: TUI gateway tool-progress mode normalizer is fixture-ready or validated, so the internal/tuigateway package directory already exists.
Pure-string transformation only — table tests with synthetic inputs.
not_ready_when: The slice opens a real home directory, calls os.Stat, or talks to the live filesystem.
The slice adds remote completion transport or a JSON-RPC handler.
acceptance: TestNormalizeCompletionPath_TildeExpansion converts ~/projects/x to /home/operator/projects/x with injected home.
TestNormalizeCompletionPath_WindowsToMntOnLinux converts C:/Users/x to /mnt/c/Users/x when isWindows=false.
TestNormalizeCompletionPath_NoExpansionOnWindows leaves C:/Users/x as C:/Users/x when isWindows=true.
TestNormalizeCompletionPath_EmptyInputReturnsEmpty without panic.
TestNormalizeCompletionPath_NoSlashConversionWithoutDriveColon leaves /usr/bin unchanged on Linux.
Helper does not call os.UserHomeDir, os.Stat, or runtime.GOOS — caller injects.
items.note: Complete: internal/tuigateway/completion_path.go exposes NormalizeCompletionPath(in string, isWindows bool) string and a stubbable package-level HomeFn hook. Tilde-only and tilde-slash forms are rewritten through the injected home; on non-Windows callers, expanded paths matching <letter>:/... are rewritten to /mnt/<lower-letter>/... (with backslash-to-slash normalisation gating the pattern check). Empty input returns empty without consulting HomeFn. The helper performs no filesystem I/O and never reads os.UserHomeDir, os.Stat, or runtime.GOOS — verified by table-driven tests in internal/tuigateway/completion_path_test.go covering TildeExpansion, BareTildeExpandsToHome, WindowsToMntOnLinux, BackslashWindowsToMntOnLinux, DriveLetterLowercased, NoExpansionOnWindows, EmptyInputReturnsEmpty, NoSlashConversionWithoutDriveColon, NonTildeNonDrivePassthrough, TildeOnWindowsStillExpands, and DriveColonWithoutSlashUntouched.
done_signal: internal/tuigateway/completion_path_test.go fixtures prove tilde expansion and Windows→/mnt rewrite without runtime.GOOS or os.UserHomeDir calls.

provenance.note: Hermes defines the WSL-friendly path normalization; Gormes ports it as an inert helper before completion transport.

items.name: TUI gateway tool summary formatter
items.contract: Pure helper internal/tuigateway/tool_summary.go exposes FormatToolSummary(name string, resultJSON []byte, durationSeconds *float64) (string, bool). Mirrors upstream tui_gateway/server.py:_tool_summary (line 996): for web_search returns 'Did N searches' (or 'Did 1 search'); for web_extract returns 'Extracted N pages'; for any other tool returns 'Completed' if duration is set, else ("", false). The duration suffix ' in 1.2s' is appended whenever durationSeconds is non-nil. Caller decides whether to emit; this helper never logs or writes.
items.degraded_mode: TUI tool-complete bubbles fall back to the raw tool name until this formatter is fixture-backed.

acceptance: TestFormatToolSummary_WebSearchPlural with `{"data":{"web":[{},{},{}]}}` returns 'Did 3 searches in 1.2s'.
TestFormatToolSummary_WebSearchSingular with one-result JSON returns 'Did 1 search'.
TestFormatToolSummary_WebExtractPlural with `{"results":[{},{}]}` returns 'Extracted 2 pages in 0.5s'.
TestFormatToolSummary_UnknownToolWithDuration returns 'Completed in 0.3s' and ok=true.
TestFormatToolSummary_UnknownToolNoDuration returns ('', false).
TestFormatToolSummary_InvalidJSONFallsBackToCompleted treats malformed result as no-data.
Helper does not depend on time.Now or external services.
items.note: Complete: internal/tuigateway/tool_summary.go exposes FormatToolSummary(name string, resultJSON []byte, durationSeconds *float64) (string, bool) as a pure JSON-shape formatter with no registry, logging, wall-clock, or service dependency. Fixtures in internal/tuigateway/tool_summary_test.go prove web_search singular/plural summaries, web_extract plural summaries, unknown-tool duration fallback, unknown-tool no-duration suppression, and malformed JSON fallback to Completed when a duration is present.

items.name: TUI prompt-submit auto-title eligibility helper
items.contract: Pure helper internal/tui/auto_title.go exposes type AutoTitleInput struct{SessionKey string; FallbackSessionID string; Status string; UserText string; AssistantText string; Interrupted bool; HistoryCount int}, type AutoTitleRequest struct{SessionID string; UserText string; AssistantText string; HistoryCount int}, and BuildAutoTitleRequest(in AutoTitleInput) (AutoTitleRequest, bool). It returns ok=true only when Status=="complete", Interrupted is false, strings.TrimSpace(UserText) and strings.TrimSpace(AssistantText) are non-empty, and the resolved session ID is non-empty. Resolution prefers strings.TrimSpace(SessionKey) and falls back to strings.TrimSpace(FallbackSessionID). The returned request preserves the original UserText/AssistantText bytes and HistoryCount. No title generation, provider call, DB write, goroutine, clock lookup, or TUI transport change in this slice.
items.degraded_mode: TUI/session status can report auto_title_skipped with reason interrupted, empty_prompt, empty_response, non_complete, or missing_session before a later row wires title generation.
ready_when: internal/tui already owns pure helper files and tests; this row adds one helper without touching Bubble Tea update flow.
The worker can table-test eligibility with synthetic strings and history counts; no title model, database, or live TUI session is required.
not_ready_when: The slice calls an LLM/title generator, writes session metadata, starts goroutines, or changes kernel/TUI submit behavior.
The slice ports Hermes Python session_key storage directly instead of adapting to Gormes SessionID metadata.
acceptance: TestBuildAutoTitleRequest_CompletePromptReturnsRequest: status complete with session_key, non-empty user text, non-empty assistant text, and HistoryCount=2 returns ok=true and preserves the original texts.
TestBuildAutoTitleRequest_FallbackSessionID: empty SessionKey with FallbackSessionID="sid" returns request.SessionID="sid".
TestBuildAutoTitleRequest_SkipsInterrupted: Interrupted=true returns ok=false even when texts are non-empty.
TestBuildAutoTitleRequest_SkipsEmptyPromptOrResponse: whitespace-only UserText or AssistantText returns ok=false.
TestBuildAutoTitleRequest_SkipsNonCompleteOrMissingSession: non-complete status or empty resolved session returns ok=false.
items.note: Tracks Hermes 9662e321, which wired maybe_auto_title into tui_gateway prompt.submit after message.complete for clean non-empty TUI turns. Gormes should land the pure eligibility/request helper first so a later wiring row can update native session titles without hiding DB/provider side effects inside the TUI update loop.
done_signal: internal/tui/auto_title_test.go fixtures prove complete-turn eligibility, interrupted/empty/non-complete skips, session-key fallback, and zero title generation side effects.

provenance.note: Hermes defines the TUI auto-title trigger; Gormes adapts it as a pure native TUI/session request helper before title generation is wired.

items.name: Native TUI slash-command dispatch table
items.contract: internal/tui exposes a generic slash-command registry — a single dispatch helper checks editor input on Enter, routes any registered slash to a handler that returns (handled bool, statusMessage string, cmd tea.Cmd), and short-circuits the normal kernel.Submit path; the existing /mouse handler migrates onto the registry, and `/save` registers as an explicit no-op stub that emits `save not yet implemented` so a follow-up row can wire the export call
items.degraded_mode: Unknown slash commands are still submitted to the model (current behavior); registered slashes that return handled=false continue submission so a buggy handler can never silently drop a turn.
ready_when: internal/tui/update.go currently switches on `tea.KeyEnter`, calls parseMouseTrackingSlash, and either short-circuits or calls submitCmd — the dispatch helper drops in at the same point.
internal/tui/mouse_tracking.go already returns a typed result struct; the new registry signature should be source-compatible so the migration is mechanical.
not_ready_when: The slice opens a network socket, talks to api_server, performs a real session export, or removes the existing mouse-slash test.
The slice changes kernel.Submit signatures or introduces a new `slash` package outside internal/tui.
unblocks: Native TUI /branch session fork + transcript target switch
TUI running-agent placeholder surfaces interrupt + queued slash actions
acceptance: internal/tui/slash_dispatch.go declares: type SlashHandler func(input string, model *Model) SlashResult; type SlashResult struct{ Handled bool, StatusMessage string, Cmd tea.Cmd }; type SlashRegistry struct{ ... }; func NewDefaultSlashRegistry() *SlashRegistry; func (r *SlashRegistry) Dispatch(input string, model *Model) SlashResult; func (r *SlashRegistry) Register(name string, handler SlashHandler).
TestSlashRegistry_DispatchRoutesToRegisteredHandler proves dispatching `/foo bar` invokes the registered `foo` handler with the full input string and returns Handled=true.
TestSlashRegistry_UnknownSlashIsNotHandled proves dispatching `/unknown` returns Handled=false so Update falls through to the normal submit path.
TestSlashRegistry_SaveStubReturnsNotImplemented proves the default registry contains a `save` entry that returns Handled=true with StatusMessage=`save not yet implemented` and a nil Cmd.

TestSlashRegistry_MouseHandlerMigrationParity proves invoking `/mouse on`, `/mouse off`, and an invalid `/mouse foo` through the registry produces the same StatusMessage values that parseMouseTrackingSlash returned in the existing mouse_tracking_test.go cases.
internal/tui/update.go's tea.KeyEnter branch calls registry.Dispatch BEFORE m.submitCmd; only handler-returning Handled=false reaches submitCmd.
items.note: Reshaped 2026-04-26 (split from `Native TUI /save canonical session export`). Original /save row was quarantined no_progress x3 because Gormes TUI has no slash-command dispatch at all — only parseMouseTrackingSlash hardcoded in update.go. Workers couldn't decide where to register a new handler. This prerequisite row introduces a generic registry and a /save no-op stub; the follow-up row replaces the stub with the export call.
done_signal: All 5 TestSlashRegistry_* fixtures pass and the existing mouse-tracking tests still pass after parseMouseTrackingSlash is migrated behind the registry.

items.name: Native TUI /save canonical session export
items.contract: Native Bubble Tea TUI replaces the /save no-op stub registered by `Native TUI slash-command dispatch table` with a handler that calls an injected SessionExportFunc(ctx, sessionID) (path string, err error) which delegates to internal/transcript.ExportMarkdown over the persisted session DB and renders the resulting file path through StatusMessage — never via the kernel submit path
items.degraded_mode: TUI StatusMessage reports `save: no conversation`, `save: no active session`, `save: store unavailable`, or `save: write failed: <err>` instead of sending /save to the model or writing partial UI-only transcripts; failed writes remove partial files via os.Remove before reporting.
ready_when: `Native TUI slash-command dispatch table` is complete (the registry and /save stub already exist).
internal/transcript.ExportMarkdown is the canonical persisted-transcript reader — `cmd/gormes/session.go` already proves it works over a sql.DB pointing at config.MemoryDBPath().
not_ready_when: The slice writes UI-shaped Model.frame.History rows directly instead of going through internal/transcript.ExportMarkdown.
The slice opens a network connection, starts SSE/api_server transport, or treats /save as a normal model prompt.
The slice changes kernel.Submit signatures or the registry shape introduced by the prior row.
acceptance: internal/tui declares: type SessionExportFunc func(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (path string, err error); Options gains a SessionExport SessionExportFunc field; NewModelWithOptions stores it on Model; the registry's `save` handler invokes it with the active session id from m.frame.SessionID (or returns `save: no active session` when empty).
TestSlashSave_NoConversationReturnsStatus runs /save against a Model with empty frame.History and asserts StatusMessage=`save: no conversation` with no SessionExportFunc call.
TestSlashSave_NoActiveSessionReturnsStatus runs /save with non-empty history but empty SessionID and asserts StatusMessage=`save: no active session`.

TestSlashSave_HappyPathReturnsWrittenPath wires a fake SessionExportFunc returning `/tmp/sess-export-fixture.md` and asserts StatusMessage contains that path, exactly one fake invocation, and no kernel.Submit call.

TestSlashSave_ExportFailureRemovesPartialFile wires a fake that writes a partial file then returns an error; asserts the partial file is removed (os.Stat ENOENT) and StatusMessage starts with `save: write failed:`.
TestSlashSave_ErrSessionNotFoundReturnsStoreUnavailable wires a fake returning transcript.ErrSessionNotFound; asserts StatusMessage=`save: store unavailable`.
items.note: Reshaped 2026-04-26 (continuation of `Native TUI slash-command dispatch table`). Wires the no-op /save stub to internal/transcript.ExportMarkdown via an injected function so unit tests do not need a real SQLite DB. cmd/gormes/main.go is responsible for binding the real implementation (open MemoryDBPath, call ExportMarkdown) when constructing the Model — that wiring lives outside this row's write_scope.
done_signal: All 5 TestSlashSave_* fixtures pass; the existing TestSlashRegistry_SaveStubReturnsNotImplemented assertion is updated or removed because the stub is replaced; no test in internal/tui opens a real network or DB connection.

provenance.note: Hermes defines /save as a JSON-RPC session.save call; Gormes wires it directly to internal/transcript.ExportMarkdown via an injected SessionExportFunc to keep the TUI testable without SSE/JSON-RPC.

items.name: Native TUI /branch session fork + transcript target switch
items.contract: Native Bubble Tea TUI adds a `/branch [name]` slash handler using an injected SessionBranchFunc(ctx context.Context, req BranchRequest) (BranchResult, error). BranchRequest carries ParentSessionID, Title, and HistoryCount; BranchResult carries SessionID, ParentSessionID, Title, and TranscriptCopied. The handler refuses empty conversation history, never submits `/branch` to the kernel, calls the injected function with the current frame SessionID, updates the model's active SessionID to the returned branch SessionID, clears per-session pending UI state, and ensures subsequent turns persist under the branch session rather than the parent. The backing session/transcript helper copies persisted turns from parent to child and writes session.Metadata with ParentSessionID=parent and LineageKind=session.LineageKindFork; ResolveLineageTip must continue ignoring fork children.
items.degraded_mode: TUI StatusMessage reports `branch: no conversation`, `branch: no active session`, `branch: store unavailable`, or `branch: fork failed: <err>`; failures leave the parent session active and do not copy partial turns.
ready_when: Native TUI slash-command dispatch table is complete, so `/branch` can register as a handled slash command instead of entering the model prompt.
parent_session_id lineage for compression splits is validated, so fork children can be represented without changing compression-tip resolution.
internal/transcript already stores persisted turns by session_id and can be exercised through sqlite fixtures with no live provider.
not_ready_when: The slice changes cmd/gormes startup wiring, opens a provider connection, or implements a full session browser/list UI in the same change.
The slice treats fork children as compression continuations or rewrites parent transcript history instead of copying to a child session.
acceptance: TestSessionFork_CopiesTranscriptAndWritesForkMetadata seeds a parent transcript, forks to a child, asserts copied turn count, ParentSessionID=parent, and LineageKindFork.
TestSessionFork_ResolveLineageTipIgnoresFork proves a fork child does not become the live compression continuation for its parent.
TestSlashBranch_EmptyHistoryReturnsStatus runs `/branch` with no model history and asserts StatusMessage=`branch: no conversation` and no SessionBranchFunc call.

TestSlashBranch_HappyPathSwitchesSessionIDAndDoesNotSubmit wires a fake SessionBranchFunc, asserts the model SessionID changes to the child, the fake sees the parent ID, and kernel.Submit is not called.
TestSlashBranch_CustomNamePreserved passes `/branch refactor path` and asserts BranchRequest.Title=`refactor path`.
items.note: Tracks Hermes 0824ba6a, which fixed /branch so post-branch writes target the branch session log and branch sessions remain visible in session lists while delegate/compression children stay hidden. Gormes adapts this to native session IDs, transcript copying, and fork lineage rather than Python session_log_file paths.
done_signal: Fork fixtures prove transcript copy, fork lineage metadata, compression-tip exclusion, TUI session switch, custom branch title propagation, and no kernel submission for `/branch`.

provenance.note: Hermes defines branch/fork session semantics; Gormes adapts them to Go-native transcript/session stores and the internal LineageKindFork contract.

items.name: TUI running-agent placeholder surfaces interrupt + queued slash actions
items.contract: internal/tui Model.editor.Placeholder switches between an idle placeholder (`Type a message and hit Enter…`) and an in-flight placeholder that lists discoverable busy-time actions — at minimum `msg=interrupt · Ctrl+C cancel`, plus any slash command registered with BusyAvailable=true on the SlashRegistry — so operators can discover the cancel + interrupt UX without reading docs
items.degraded_mode: If the registry exposes no busy-available slashes, the in-flight placeholder degrades to `msg=interrupt · Ctrl+C cancel` only (Gormes's current minimum-viable busy actions).
ready_when: `Native TUI slash-command dispatch table` is complete (the registry is the source of truth for which slashes show in the busy placeholder).
internal/tui/update.go already toggles `m.inFlight` between user submit and PhaseIdle frame; this slice piggybacks on that boolean.
not_ready_when: The slice introduces /queue, /bg, or /steer slash commands themselves — those are separate larger rows; this slice only surfaces them in the placeholder IF they exist as registered busy-available slashes.
The slice changes Ctrl+C semantics, the cancel callback signature, or the kernel.Submit path.
The slice writes to stdout/stderr instead of the Bubble Tea editor placeholder.
acceptance: internal/tui/slash_dispatch.go's SlashRegistry exposes BusyAvailable on Register: `Register(name string, handler SlashHandler, opts ...RegisterOpt)` with `WithBusyAvailable() RegisterOpt`; the default `save` stub registers WITHOUT BusyAvailable.

internal/tui/running_placeholder.go declares `func (m Model) RunningPlaceholder() string` that returns `Type a message and hit Enter…` when m.inFlight is false, and otherwise returns `msg=interrupt · ` + comma-joined registry.BusyAvailableSlashes() with `/` prefixes + ` · Ctrl+C cancel` (no trailing/leading whitespace beyond the joiners).

internal/tui/update.go's frameMsg branch sets `m.editor.Placeholder = m.RunningPlaceholder()` after the PhaseIdle/in-flight transition is decided, NOT before, so the placeholder reflects the post-frame state.
TestRunningPlaceholder_IdleStateUsesIdleText asserts the placeholder is `Type a message and hit Enter…` when inFlight=false.
TestRunningPlaceholder_BusyStateListsRegisteredBusySlashes registers two fake busy slashes (`queue`, `steer`) and asserts the placeholder is `msg=interrupt · /queue · /steer · Ctrl+C cancel`.
TestRunningPlaceholder_BusyStateWithNoBusySlashesShowsMinimum asserts a registry with zero busy-available slashes produces `msg=interrupt · Ctrl+C cancel`.
items.note: Tracks Hermes commit eaa7e2db (2026-04-26) which surfaced /queue · /bg · /steer · Ctrl+C cancel in cli.py's running placeholder. Gormes does not have /queue/bg/steer slash commands yet — this row only adds the placeholder mechanism (gated on the SlashRegistry); each individual busy slash command is a separate downstream row.
done_signal: All 3 TestRunningPlaceholder_* fixtures pass; the placeholder transitions on PhaseIdle frames; no Hermes /queue or /bg implementation is added by this slice.

provenance.note: Hermes shipped /queue + /bg + /steer slash commands first then surfaced them in cli.py's placeholder. Gormes inverts the order: the placeholder mechanism lands now (gated on SlashRegistry) so each future busy slash registers itself and is discovered automatically.

drift_state.origin_decision: Hermes still leads API server, remote TUI, TUI launch-model, selection/copy, slash-command, and dashboard contract discovery, but Gormes owns the native Go Bubble Tea/API implementation. Hermes ee0728c6 stays an explicit no-Node divergence row, 283c8fd6 adds TUI model override/static alias behavior, edc78e25/31d7f195 add Ink selection-copy fixes that Gormes documents as a terminal-native divergence, 2536a36f routes TUI /save through canonical session.save JSON-RPC, 0824ba6a tightens /branch so post-branch writes target the branch session and branch children remain visible without becoming compression continuations, and 9662e321 wires prompt.submit auto-title generation after clean non-empty TUI completions. Gormes tracks those as native /save, /branch, and auto-title eligibility rows over internal/transcript, session.LineageKindFork, and native TUI helper seams instead of importing Hermes Python session_log_file or maybe_auto_title wiring.

items.name: Execution-mode resolver + config precedence
items.note: TDD slice 1 of 4: pure helper in `internal/tools/execute_code.go` that returns `off`/`project`/`strict` from (a) env var (e.g. `GORMES_EXECUTE_CODE_MODE`), then (b) config key, then (c) default, with unknown values falling back to default and emitting a warning. Table tests only — no sandbox coupling, no CWD/interpreter behavior yet. Freeze the token set here so later slices cannot silently add modes.

items.name: Strict-mode CWD + interpreter parity
items.note: TDD slice 2 of 4: strict mode keeps today's behavior frozen — isolated tmpdir CWD plus the canonical interpreter resolved via `LocalCodeSandbox.resolveRuntime`. Fixtures must prove the 5.K blocked-result envelope (status, error, filesystem_access, network_access) is unchanged and that relative imports/filesystem access outside the tmpdir continue to fail. This slice exists only to pin the reference behavior before project-mode lands.

items.name: Project-mode CWD + active venv detection
items.note: TDD slice 3 of 4: port upstream `_resolve_python_binary`/`_resolve_cwd` project semantics — session's working directory for CWD and the active virtualenv/conda python when present, falling back to the resolved system interpreter. Unit tests must drive resolution via injected env (`VIRTUAL_ENV`, `CONDA_PREFIX`), not the real host. Do not widen the 5.K filesystem/network blocking contract — project mode still flows through the same `sandboxGuardReason` gate.

items.name: Default mode selection + config cut-over
items.note: TDD slice 4 of 4: pick and document Gormes' default mode (recommend `strict` given the self-hosted security-first posture, with `project` opt-in via config). Wire the resolver into `NewExecuteCodeTool` so callers get the right mode without per-call arguments, add a regression test that the 5.K `Sandboxed exec` blocked-result envelope is byte-identical across modes, and update the rationale in `docs/content/building-gormes/core-systems/tool-execution.md` so operators understand the trade-off before flipping the default.

6.name: Phase 6 — The Learning Loop (Soul)
6.deliverable: Native skill extraction. Compounding intelligence. The feature Hermes doesn't have.

items.name: Heuristic or LLM-scored signal
items.note: TDD: start with deterministic local signals from transcript length, tool-call count, retries, edits, and operator feedback before adding any LLM scorer, so learning-loop triggers are explainable.

items.name: LLM-assisted pattern distillation
items.contract: Skill distillation review prompts survey existing skills first, prefer class-level generalization over near-duplicate creation, and preserve a clear no-op escape path before any generated draft can be promoted
items.degraded_mode: Skill review status reports review_prompt_unavailable, overlap_detected, no_skill_to_save, or draft_requires_review instead of auto-promoting narrow one-off skills.
ready_when: Portable SKILL.md format is validated, so generated drafts can carry review state and remain out of prompt injection until approved.
The row can use fake model/review prompt fixtures; no live provider, skill install, or prompt injection is required.
not_ready_when: The slice creates active skills directly, calls a live LLM, or changes the skill selector before the prompt/review contract is fixture-locked.
The slice writes broad skill files without overlap evidence or a class-level task name.
acceptance: Prompt fixtures instruct the reviewer to call skills_list and inspect likely existing skills before deciding.
Prompt fixtures explicitly prefer updating/generalizing an existing skill at the task-class level and gate new skill creation as a last resort.
Overlap evidence is captured for follow-up review when a draft resembles existing skills.
The no-op escape phrase `Nothing to save.` remains valid and produces no draft writes.
items.note: Hermes 76042f58 changed skill review prompts to be class-first and update-over-create. Gormes should fixture-lock that review contract before adding any LLM-backed skill extractor.
done_signal: Distillation prompt fixtures prove survey-first behavior, class-level generalization, overlap evidence, draft-review gating, and the Nothing-to-save escape path.

provenance.note: Hermes defines the class-first review prompt behavior; Gormes adapts it to native skill distillation and review-state storage.

items.name: Portable SKILL.md format
items.contract: Reviewed skill-as-code storage format
items.degraded_mode: Skill status excludes unreviewed or invalid drafts from prompt injection and records resolver or metadata failures.

ready_when: Phase 2.G skills runtime is complete and the parser/store seam is stable enough for versioned metadata.
not_ready_when: Generated drafts are allowed into prompt injection without explicit review metadata.
acceptance: SKILL.md metadata includes version, provenance, review state, triggers, and exclusions.
Invalid, disabled, or unreviewed drafts never enter prompt injection.
Atomic writes preserve human-editable skill files and version history.
items.note: TDD: extend the Phase 2.G parser/store contract with versioned metadata, provenance, review state, and atomic writes while preserving human-editable SKILL.md files.
done_signal: SKILL.md metadata fixtures prove invalid, disabled, or unreviewed drafts stay out of prompt injection.

items.name: Hybrid lexical + semantic lookup
items.contract: Reviewed skills are selected by a deterministic lexical plus semantic scorer while disabled and unreviewed drafts remain excluded from prompt injection
items.degraded_mode: Skill status reports semantic lookup unavailable or review-gated instead of silently injecting low-confidence or unreviewed skills.
ready_when: Portable SKILL.md format is validated so skills carry review state, triggers, exclusions, and provenance metadata.
Phase 3 semantic embedding lookup remains optional and can be stubbed in unit fixtures without Ollama.
not_ready_when: The slice injects disabled/unreviewed skills, requires live embeddings in unit tests, or ports GBrain's PGLite/Postgres search runtime.
unblocks: Code Cathedral II code-context retrieval fixtures
acceptance: Lexical trigger/exclusion scores and semantic scores combine into a stable explanation per selected skill.
Disabled, invalid, and unreviewed skills are excluded before scoring and reported in status evidence.
Missing semantic embeddings degrade to lexical-only selection with an explicit reason.
items.note: TDD: combine lexical tags with Phase 3 semantic embeddings behind a deterministic scorer, using GBrain f718c59 retrieval lessons as donor evidence without importing its database/runtime.
done_signal: Skill lookup fixtures prove deterministic lexical+semantic selection, review gating, semantic degraded mode, and score explanations.

provenance.note: GBrain informs retrieval scoring; Gormes adapts it to reviewed SKILL.md assets and SQLite/optional embeddings.

items.name: Source-aware retrieval damping fixtures
items.contract: Skill and memory retrieval can prefer curated/reviewed sources over bulky chat or raw imports while preserving high-detail temporal lookup and explaining every source boost or exclusion
items.degraded_mode: Retrieval status reports source_boost_unavailable, source_excluded, source_dampened, or temporal_bypass evidence instead of silently letting transcript bulk swamp curated knowledge.
ready_when: Hybrid lexical + semantic lookup is validated and returns score explanations that can carry source-tier evidence.
The worker can use synthetic skill/memory sources and fake scores; no GBrain SQL engine, PGLite, Postgres, or tree-sitter runtime is required.
not_ready_when: The slice copies GBrain SQL builders, introduces Postgres/PGLite, or applies hard excludes without operator-visible evidence.
The slice dampens high-detail temporal/history queries that should intentionally search chat-like sources.
unblocks: Code Cathedral II code-context retrieval fixtures
acceptance: Synthetic curated/reviewed skill or memory entries outrank bulk transcript/raw-import entries when lexical and semantic scores are otherwise close.
Configured hard-exclude source prefixes are removed before ranking and reported in source_excluded evidence.
High-detail or temporal lookup mode bypasses damping so recent chat/history sources can still surface.
Score explanations include source tier, boost/damping factor, and bypass/exclusion reason without importing GBrain's SQL runtime.
items.note: GBrain 172b55b/v0.22.0 added source-aware ranking to prevent chat/source swamp. Gormes should borrow the source-tier idea for reviewed skills and memory retrieval explanations, not the Postgres/PGLite SQL implementation.
done_signal: Source-aware retrieval fixtures prove curated/reviewed boost, bulk-source damping, hard-exclude evidence, temporal bypass, and score explanations without GBrain runtime imports.

provenance.note: GBrain defines source-swamp resistance; Gormes adapts it as explainable source-tier scoring for skills and memory.

items.name: Code Cathedral II code-context retrieval fixtures
items.contract: Learning-loop retrieval can consume code-context evidence with parent-scope and call-edge explanations without importing GBrain's TypeScript indexer
items.degraded_mode: Skill retrieval reports code-context unavailable rather than pretending call-graph or parent-scope evidence exists.
ready_when: Hybrid lexical + semantic lookup is validated and can accept optional external evidence in fixtures.
not_ready_when: The slice embeds tree-sitter WASM, ports GBrain's reindex-code CLI, creates a separate code database, or runs repository-wide indexing in unit tests.
acceptance: Synthetic code-context fixtures carry symbol, parent scope, and call-edge fields into skill retrieval explanations.
High-fan-out call edges are capped deterministically and reported as capped evidence.
When code context is absent or stale, skill lookup falls back to lexical/semantic scoring with explicit degraded evidence.
items.note: GBrain f718c59 Code Cathedral II adds call-graph edges, two-pass retrieval, and parent-scope chunks. Gormes should first define the tiny evidence contract a learning-loop selector can consume; a Go code indexer is a later decision.
done_signal: Code-context retrieval fixtures prove parent-scope/call-edge evidence is optional, capped, explained, and never requires a GBrain runtime import.

provenance.note: GBrain's Code Cathedral II is donor evidence for code-context retrieval, not a subsystem to embed.

drift_state.origin_decision: GBrain f718c59/172b55b lead the retrieval lessons for hybrid scoring, Code Cathedral II evidence, and source-swamp resistance. Gormes should adapt these as small SQLite/skills evidence contracts with visible degraded modes, not import GBrain's TypeScript chunker, PGLite/Postgres search runtime, or SQL ranking builders.

items.name: Skill effectiveness scoring
items.note: TDD: persist skill-use outcomes, explicit operator feedback, and decay/weight adjustments as auditable records before any automatic promotion or demotion behavior.

items.name: TUI + Telegram browsing
items.note: TDD: expose browse/edit/disable/review flows through TUI and gateway commands after the store and feedback records are stable, with permission and active-turn behavior pinned by tests.

7.name: Phase 7 — Paused Channel Backlog
7.deliverable: Deferred non-priority channel adapters after Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, and WeChat stabilize

items.name: Inbound event normalization + session identity
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/signal.NormalizeInbound now freezes Signal DM/group chat IDs, generic slash passthrough, and alternate phone/UUID session identity before client wiring.

items.name: Reply/send contract on shared chassis
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/signal.Bot now records reply metadata from normalized inbound events and routes shared-chassis sends back to direct recipients or native group IDs with reply-to preservation.

items.name: Signal transport/bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD: freeze signal-cli or bridge client lifecycle, receive-loop reconnect/error handling, and outbound attachment/send seams before binding a real transport.

items.name: Email ingress + outbound delivery contract
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/email now normalizes RFC 822 MIME bodies into plaintext gateway events and derives deterministic reply-thread targets before IMAP or SMTP transport glue.

items.name: SMS ingress + outbound delivery contract
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/sms.NormalizeInbound now canonicalizes phone-number session keys, delegates generic slash commands through gateway.ParseInboundText, and BuildDelivery splits outbound replies into natural-boundary 1600-rune segments for future Twilio sends.

items.name: Threaded text adapter contract suite
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/threadtext freezes canonical thread IDs, gateway inbound normalization, and reply-target derivation before Matrix/Mattermost transport work.

items.name: Matrix shared-chassis bot seam
items.note: TDD: land a Matrix-specific bot seam on top of `internal/channels/threadtext` that freezes canonical thread reply targets, root-message thread defaults, and placeholder/edit hooks before auth/sync/E2EE transport work.

items.name: Mattermost shared-chassis bot seam
items.note: TDD: land a Mattermost-specific bot seam on top of `internal/channels/threadtext` that freezes canonical reply targets, flat-vs-thread root reply defaults, and placeholder/edit hooks before REST/WS transport work.

items.name: Matrix real client/bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD: freeze auth, sync/invite handling, room-kind and mention policy, and media/E2EE bootstrap seams before binding a real Matrix client.

items.name: Matrix E2EE device-id crypto-store binding
items.contract: Matrix E2EE bootstrap binds the configured runtime device_id into the crypto store before account writes so Olm/megolm identity lookups stay device-scoped
items.degraded_mode: Matrix status reports E2EE unavailable or stale device identity instead of starting encrypted sync with a blank or mismatched device_id.
ready_when: Matrix real client/bootstrap layer has auth, sync/invite handling, room-kind policy, and a fakeable E2EE bootstrap seam.
not_ready_when: The slice starts a live Matrix sync, stores real keys in tests, or tries to solve cross-signing/recovery-key UX.
acceptance: Fake crypto-store tests prove put_device_id runs before first account put when a device_id is configured.
Blank or missing device_id degrades visibly and does not claim encrypted-room readiness.
Existing thread/session identity rules from internal/channels/threadtext are not changed by E2EE bootstrap.
items.note: Hermes e5647d78 fixed a Matrix PgCryptoStore blank-device trap that broke downstream Olm/megolm operations. Keep this as a separate paused Phase 7 slice so the future Go Matrix adapter does not bury E2EE device identity inside transport startup.
done_signal: Matrix crypto-store fixtures prove configured device_id is bound before account writes and missing/stale identity degrades visibly.

items.name: Mattermost REST/WS bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD: freeze auth, websocket reconnect, channel/user lookup, and file upload/edit seams before binding a real Mattermost transport.

items.name: Signed event parsing + auth gates
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/webhook.ParseInbound now enforces body-size and event-filter gates, and ValidateSignature freezes GitHub, GitLab, and generic HMAC auth before any HTTP runtime wiring.

items.name: Prompt-to-delivery routing bridge
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/webhook now renders prompt and deliver_extra templates, derives deterministic webhook session chat IDs, and resolves typed gateway delivery targets including thread aliases.

items.name: BlueBubbles + HomeAssistant adapters
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/bluebubbles and internal/channels/homeassistant now freeze webhook-auth, cached home-channel delivery, filtered state-change formatting, cooldown, and persistent-notification contracts before any SDK wiring.

items.name: BlueBubbles iMessage bubble formatting parity
items.contract: BlueBubbles outbound iMessage sends are non-editable, markdown-stripped, paragraph-split bubbles without pagination suffixes
items.degraded_mode: BlueBubbles remains a usable first-pass adapter, but long replies may still arrive as one stripped text send until paragraph splitting and suffix-free chunking are fixture-locked.
ready_when: The first-pass BlueBubbles adapter already owns Send, markdown stripping, cached GUID resolution, and home-channel fallback in internal/channels/bluebubbles.
not_ready_when: The slice attempts to add live BlueBubbles HTTP/webhook registration, attachment download, reactions, typing indicators, or edit-message support.
acceptance: Send splits blank-line-separated paragraphs into separate SendText calls while preserving existing chat GUID resolution and home-channel fallback.
Long paragraph chunks omit `(n/m)` pagination suffixes and concatenate back to the stripped original text.
Bot does not implement gateway.MessageEditor or gateway.PlaceholderCapable, preserving non-editable iMessage semantics.
items.note: Upstream Hermes f731c2c2 changed BlueBubbles to `SUPPORTS_MESSAGE_EDITING = False`, split blank-line paragraphs into separate iMessage bubbles, and removed pagination suffixes from chunked sends. Gormes' shipped first-pass adapter has the right package boundary but still sends one stripped body, so this is a narrow parity slice.
done_signal: BlueBubbles adapter tests prove paragraph-to-bubble sends, suffix-free chunking, and no edit/placeholder capability.

items.name: Feishu shared-chassis bot seam
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/feishu now pins webhook-versus-websocket ingress, group mention policy, allowlist behavior, and reply-target preservation before any SDK wiring.

items.name: DingTalk shared-chassis bot seam
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/dingtalk.Bot now covers allow-list policy, mention-gated ingress, generic command parsing, session-webhook capture, and callback-bound reply delivery; runtime/bootstrap and real SDK binding remain tracked separately.

items.name: QQ Bot shared-chassis bot seam
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/qqbot now covers DM/group policy gates, mention-gated ingress, and passive-reply sequencing before any runtime bootstrap.

items.name: Feishu transport/bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD: freeze webhook and websocket client lifecycle, credential validation, and rich-text send failure modes before binding a real Feishu transport.

items.name: Feishu drive-comment rule + pairing seam
items.note: TDD: port the upstream `gateway/platforms/feishu_comment_rules.py` contract as a Go rule resolver with exact document, wildcard, and top-level fallback plus allowlist/pairing decisions before comment replies can invoke an agent.

items.name: Feishu drive-comment reply workflow
items.note: TDD: freeze `drive.notice.comment_add_v1` parsing, OK-reaction behavior, whole-document vs local-thread prompt construction, and reply fallback semantics from `gateway/platforms/feishu_comment.py` before real SDK binding.

items.name: DingTalk transport/bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD landed: internal/channels/dingtalk now freezes Stream Mode bootstrap via DecideRuntime plus SessionWebhooks refresh and ReplySender retry/error contracts before any real SDK binding.

items.name: DingTalk real SDK binding
items.note: TDD: bind the frozen Stream Mode/runtime contract to the real DingTalk SDK, credential checks, receive loop, and send lifecycle without widening the already-tested session-webhook retry seam.

items.name: DingTalk AI Cards streaming-update contract
items.note: TDD landed: AICardBot now freezes the SDK-neutral DingTalk AI Card create/deliver/streaming-update contract with fixture-backed tests, uses gateway.FinalizingMessageEditor for terminal-frame finalize semantics, and falls back to the stored session webhook when card streaming updates fail. Real SDK binding remains tracked separately.

items.name: DingTalk emoji reaction send/receive parity
items.note: TDD landed: EmojiReactionClient now freezes DingTalk robot emotion reply/recall payloads with fixture-backed tests, emits Thinking on accepted inbound messages, swaps to Done on final session-webhook and AI Card replies, and keeps reaction failures as best-effort so fallback delivery still succeeds. Real SDK binding remains tracked separately.

items.name: DingTalk media (image/file) attachment routing
items.note: TDD landed: DingTalk media routing now keeps image/file download-code handling SDK-neutral, resolves robot message file-download requests into gateway attachments with fixture-backed tests, preserves media-only turns, and falls back to the original DingTalk download code with an error note when URL resolution fails. Real SDK binding remains tracked separately.

items.name: QQ Bot transport/bootstrap layer
items.note: TDD: freeze gateway/event receive loops, passive-reply lifecycle, and platform-specific send failure handling before binding a real QQ transport.
